- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	"""Runner for the query-gen COVERAGE-ISOLATION bake-off (I-qgen-001, GH #1291).
2	
3	Wires the pure harness (qgen_coverage_harness + qgen_methods) to REAL POLARIS retrieval
4	and a REAL GLM-5.2 coverage judge, then ranks the query-gen methods by required-point
5	coverage on a canonical DRB-II task. NO report generation / rendering / DeepTRACE judge —
6	this scores ONLY query generation, on the COVERAGE axis it drives.
7	
8	Two modes:
9	  --dry-run  : a deterministic STUB world (no API spend). Proves the wiring end-to-end and
10	               that the harness ranks correctly. Default.
11	  --real     : real Serper/S2 retrieval + real GLM-5.2 judge. Requires the env gate
12	               PG_QGEN_AUTHORIZED_SPEND=1 (the one wall Claude does NOT self-authorize) and
13	               the usual run keys (.env). Every retrieved query is cached to disk keyed by
14	               sha256(query) so every method sees IDENTICAL retrieval (isolation) and re-runs
15	               are free.
16	
17	The floor method's queries are POLARIS's CURRENT query-gen output (anchor + decomposed
18	sub-queries via query_decomposer) — the real baseline every candidate must beat.
19	"""
20	
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import argparse
24	import asyncio
25	import hashlib
26	import json
27	import os
28	import sys
29	
30	from scripts.dr_benchmark.gate0_lineage import SLUG_TO_IDX
31	from scripts.dr_benchmark.qgen_coverage_harness import (
32	    CoverageBudget,
33	    load_required_points,
34	    run_coverage_test,
35	)
36	from scripts.dr_benchmark.qgen_methods import ClosedLoopMethod, FloorMethod
37	
38	_CACHE_DIR = os.path.join("outputs", "qgen_coverage", "retrieval_cache")
39	# Bump when the cached row schema or retrieval semantics change, so a stale cache cannot
40	# silently feed a new run. Part of the cache key alongside the query + domain.
41	_CACHE_SCHEMA_VERSION = "v1"
42	
43	
44	def _qkey(query: str, domain: str | None) -> str:
45	    """Cache key = sha256(schema_version | domain | normalized query). Including domain +
46	    version stops cross-domain or post-change cache reuse from contaminating a real run."""
47	    payload = f"{_CACHE_SCHEMA_VERSION}|{domain or ''}|{query.strip().lower()}"
48	    return hashlib.sha256(payload.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()[:24]
49	
50	
51	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- floor queries
52	def _sweep_query_spec(slug: str) -> dict:
53	    """The SWEEP_QUERIES spec dict for a slug (from run_honest_sweep_r3) — fail loud if absent."""
54	    from scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3 import SWEEP_QUERIES
55	
56	    for spec in SWEEP_QUERIES:
57	        if isinstance(spec, dict) and spec.get("slug") == slug:
58	            return spec
59	    raise SystemExit(
60	        f"[qgen] floor: slug {slug!r} not found in SWEEP_QUERIES — cannot build the real "
61	        f"POLARIS floor query set. Add the slug, or this task is not a sweep query."
62	    )
63	
64	
65	def floor_queries(slug: str, question: str) -> list[str]:
66	    """POLARIS's CURRENT query-gen FACETS for a benchmark slug (the floor under test).
67	
68	    The live sweep builds the effective list as anchor + build_amplified_query_list(
69	    hand_authored=q["amplified"], decomposed=decompose_question(question), regulatory, trial)
70	    (run_honest_sweep_r3.py ~L6156). The hand-authored "amplified" set is the BULK of the real
71	    floor, so we pull it from SWEEP_QUERIES by slug — benchmarking the ACTUAL current POLARIS
72	    query-gen, not a crippled decompose-only floor (Codex iter-2 P1).
73	
74	    Scope: regulatory/trial expansions are DOMAIN-BACKEND augmentations (a retrieval-section
75	    concern), deliberately excluded so this isolation test measures query GENERATION, not the
76	    domain backends (a separate section's bake-off). The anchor question is added by FloorMethod,
77	    so it is NOT in the returned facet list.
78	    """
79	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.query_decomposer import (
80	        build_amplified_query_list,
81	        decompose_question,
82	    )
83	
84	    spec = _sweep_query_spec(slug)
85	    return build_amplified_query_list(
86	        hand_authored=list(spec.get("amplified", [])),
87	        decomposed=decompose_question(question),
88	        regulatory=[],
89	        trial=[],
90	    )
91	
92	
93	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- real retrieve
94	def _row_text(row: dict) -> str:
95	    """Assemble per-source corpus text from a POLARIS evidence row.
96	
97	    METRIC LABEL (Codex P2): this measures POLARIS GENERATOR-VISIBLE evidence coverage —
98	    title + statement + the grounding span (direct_quote) that the row actually carries forward,
99	    NOT the full fetched body. It answers "can the report ground this point on what retrieval
100	    surfaced", which is the right query-gen coverage signal. If a future test needs raw
101	    source-retrieval coverage (evidence anywhere in the fetched body, even un-quoted), persist a
102	    richer ``coverage_text`` field on the row and read it here instead.
103	    """
104	    parts = [row.get("title") or "", row.get("statement") or "", row.get("direct_quote") or ""]
105	    return " ".join(p for p in parts if p).strip()
106	
107	
108	def make_real_retrieve(domain: str | None = None):
109	    """A per-query retrieve() backed by run_live_retrieval, cached to disk by query hash."""
110	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.live_retriever import run_live_retrieval
111	
112	    os.makedirs(_CACHE_DIR, exist_ok=True)
113	
114	    def retrieve(query: str) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
115	        cache_path = os.path.join(_CACHE_DIR, f"{_qkey(query, domain)}.json")
116	        if os.path.isfile(cache_path):
117	            with open(cache_path, encoding="utf-8") as handle:
118	                return json.load(handle)
119	        result = run_live_retrieval(
120	            research_question=query, amplified_queries=[], domain=domain, anchor_seed=True
121	        )
122	        rows = [
123	            {"url": r.get("source_url") or "", "text": _row_text(r)}
124	            for r in (result.evidence_rows or [])
125	        ]
126	        with open(cache_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
127	            json.dump(rows, handle, ensure_ascii=False)
128	        return rows
129	
130	    return retrieve
131	
132	
133	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- real GLM judge
134	def make_glm_llm():
135	    """An LLM callable (prompt -> text) backed by the locked GLM-5.2 mirror backbone.
136	
137	    The model is INSTANCE-scoped on OpenRouterClient — generate(prompt, system="", max_tokens=...)
138	    has NO model/messages kwargs — so we construct the client pinned to z-ai/glm-5.2 (the lock's
139	    mirror/backbone) and call generate(prompt=...). max_tokens is set GENEROUSLY (a cap, not a
140	    target — billed by actual usage) per the repo LLM-governance rule: never starve reasoning.
141	    """
142	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient
143	
144	    model = os.getenv("PG_QGEN_BACKBONE_MODEL", "z-ai/glm-5.2")
145	    client = OpenRouterClient(model=model)
146	    max_tokens = int(os.getenv("PG_QGEN_LLM_MAX_TOKENS", "8192"))
147	
148	    def llm(prompt: str) -> str:
149	        resp = asyncio.run(client.generate(prompt=prompt, max_tokens=max_tokens))
150	        return getattr(resp, "content", "") or ""
151	
152	    return llm
153	
154	
155	def make_glm_judge_llm():
156	    """A REASONING-EXCLUDED, THREAD-SAFE GLM-5.2 callable for the fast judge + query-list generation.
157	
158	    GLM-5.2's reasoning is EFFORT-based, not token-capped — `reasoning_max_tokens` does NOT reduce
159	    it (a YES/NO call still burned ~700-2700 reasoning tokens => 5-33s each; cf.
160	    fact_mirror_blank_xhigh_effort). The right knob is `reasoning_exclude=True`: skip the reasoning
161	    pass entirely and answer directly. THREAD-SAFETY (bug fix): the coverage judge calls this from a
162	    ThreadPoolExecutor, and each call does asyncio.run() on a FRESH event loop per thread. An
163	    OpenRouterClient holds an aiohttp session bound to ONE loop, so SHARING one client across worker
164	    threads deadlocks (8 threads x 8 loops on one session => hang at ~5 calls). Fix: a THREAD-LOCAL
165	    client — every worker thread constructs its own client on first use, so no loop-bound state is
166	    shared across threads.
167	    """
168	    import threading
169	
170	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient
171	
172	    model = os.getenv("PG_QGEN_BACKBONE_MODEL", "z-ai/glm-5.2")
173	    max_tokens = int(os.getenv("PG_QGEN_JUDGE_MAX_TOKENS", "1200"))
174	    _tls = threading.local()
175	
176	    def _client() -> "OpenRouterClient":
177	        c = getattr(_tls, "client", None)
178	        if c is None:
179	            c = OpenRouterClient(model=model)
180	            _tls.client = c
181	        return c
182	
183	    def llm(prompt: str) -> str:
184	        resp = asyncio.run(
185	            _client().generate(prompt=prompt, max_tokens=max_tokens, reasoning_exclude=True)
186	        )
187	        return getattr(resp, "content", "") or ""
188	
189	    return llm
190	
191	
192	def make_glm_coverage_judge():
193	    """A coverage judge (required_point, corpus_text) -> bool, backed by GLM-5.2.
194	
195	    Asks: does the corpus contain evidence that lets a report cover this required point? Strict
196	    YES/NO; defaults to NO on ambiguity (under-claim coverage, never over-claim). The corpus is
197	    CHUNKED into windows so EVERY source is judged — never truncated to the first N chars, which
198	    let row order / truncation decide the score (Codex iter-2 P1). Returns True as soon as any
199	    chunk supports the point (short-circuit). A chunk-count cap is logged HONESTLY if hit.
200	    """
201	    llm = make_glm_llm()
202	    window = int(os.getenv("PG_QGEN_JUDGE_WINDOW_CHARS", "48000"))
203	    # A SANITY bound only — NOT a truncation point. Every chunk is judged; if a corpus is so
204	    # large it would exceed this, we FAIL LOUD rather than silently score a prefix (Codex P1-7).
205	    hard_max_chunks = int(os.getenv("PG_QGEN_JUDGE_HARD_MAX_CHUNKS", "500"))
206	
207	    def judge(point: str, corpus_text: str) -> bool:
208	        text = corpus_text or ""
209	        chunks = [text[i : i + window] for i in range(0, max(len(text), 1), window)] or [""]
210	        if len(chunks) > hard_max_chunks:
211	            raise RuntimeError(
212	                f"[qgen-judge] corpus {len(text)} chars -> {len(chunks)} chunks exceeds the "
213	                f"sanity bound {hard_max_chunks}. Refusing to silently score a truncated prefix "
214	                f"(row order must NOT decide coverage). Raise PG_QGEN_JUDGE_HARD_MAX_CHUNKS "
215	                f"(cost is fine) or investigate the corpus size."
216	            )
217	        for chunk in chunks:  # judge EVERY chunk so every source is seen; short-circuit on YES
218	            prompt = (
219	                "You are scoring RETRIEVAL COVERAGE. Given a REQUIRED point a complete report "
220	                "must cover and a CHUNK of the retrieved corpus, answer strictly YES or NO: does "
221	                "this chunk contain evidence that would let a report COVER the required point? "
222	                "Answer NO if absent, only tangential, or ambiguous.\n\n"
223	                f"REQUIRED POINT:\n{point}\n\nCORPUS CHUNK:\n{chunk}\n\n"
224	                "Answer with one word: YES or NO."
225	            )
226	            if (llm(prompt) or "").strip().upper().startswith("YES"):
227	                return True
228	        return False
229	
230	    return judge
231	
232	
233	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- stub world
234	def _stub_retrieve_and_judge():
235	    """A deterministic stub world for --dry-run (no spend): the floor reaches a couple of
236	    points; the closed loop's gap re-query reaches more. Proves wiring + ranking only."""
237	    world = {
238	        "ai": [{"url": "u/ai", "text": "generative ai labor market evidence positive views"}],
239	        "negative": [{"url": "u/neg", "text": "negative views job displacement evidence"}],
240	        "challenge": [{"url": "u/ch", "text": "specific challenges reskilling evidence"}],
241	        "opportunit": [{"url": "u/op", "text": "future opportunities new occupations evidence"}],
242	    }
243	
244	    def retrieve(query: str) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
245	        q = query.lower()
246	        out: list[dict[str, str]] = []
247	        for key, rows in world.items():
248	            if key in q:
249	                out += rows
250	        return out
251	
252	    def judge(point: str, corpus_text: str) -> bool:
253	        p = point.lower()
254	        ct = corpus_text.lower()
255	        for token in ("positive", "negative", "challenge", "opportunit"):
256	            if token in p and token in ct:
257	                return True
258	        return False
259	
260	    def stub_llm(prompt: str) -> str:
261	        if "Decompose" in prompt:
262	            return "ai labor market\nnegative views\nspecific challenges\nfuture opportunities"
263	        if "THIN or MISSING" in prompt:
264	            return "negative views\nspecific challenges\nfuture opportunities"
265	        return ""
266	
267	    return retrieve, judge, stub_llm
268	
269	
270	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- main
271	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
272	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Query-gen coverage-isolation bake-off")
273	    parser.add_argument("--slug", default="drb_72_ai_labor", help="benchmark slug (gate0)")
274	    parser.add_argument("--idx", type=int, default=None, help="override canonical idx")
275	    parser.add_argument("--real", action="store_true", help="real retrieval + GLM judge (spend)")
276	    parser.add_argument("--out", default="outputs/qgen_coverage/result.json")
277	    parser.add_argument("--domain", default=None)
278	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
279	
280	    from scripts.dr_benchmark.gate0_lineage import (
281	        DRB_SLUGS_WITHOUT_CANONICAL_GOLD,
282	        assert_drb_slug_registered,
283	        is_benchmark_slug,
284	    )
285	
286	    # Fail loud on an unregistered drb_* benchmark slug (the silent-gap class).
287	    assert_drb_slug_registered(args.slug)
288	    if args.slug in DRB_SLUGS_WITHOUT_CANONICAL_GOLD:
289	        print(
290	            f"[qgen] slug {args.slug!r} has NO DRB-II gold task (no info_recall rubric) — it "
291	            f"cannot be coverage-scored. Aborting.",
292	            file=sys.stderr,
293	        )
294	        return 2
295	    mapped = SLUG_TO_IDX.get(args.slug)
296	    if is_benchmark_slug(args.slug):
297	        # A benchmark slug is CANONICALLY bound; --idx may only CONFIRM, never override — the
298	        # id<->idx mismatch is the exact drb_72 disaster class.
299	        if args.idx is not None and args.idx != mapped:
300	            print(
301	                f"[qgen] REFUSED: --idx {args.idx} != canonical idx {mapped} for benchmark slug "
302	                f"{args.slug!r}. The id/idx mismatch is the drb_72 disaster class — drop --idx.",
303	                file=sys.stderr,
304	            )
305	            return 2
306	        idx = mapped  # not None: registration passed AND slug is not in the no-gold set
307	    else:
308	        # A non-benchmark slug has no canonical mapping; --idx is REQUIRED to pick the rubric set.
309	        if args.idx is None:
310	            print(
311	                f"[qgen] non-benchmark slug {args.slug!r} needs an explicit --idx (no canonical "
312	                f"mapping).",
313	                file=sys.stderr,
314	            )
315	            return 2
316	        idx = args.idx
317	
318	    required = load_required_points(idx)
319	    print(f"[qgen] idx={idx} slug={args.slug} required_points={len(required)} mode={'REAL' if args.real else 'DRY-RUN'}")
320	
321	    if args.real:
322	        if os.getenv("PG_QGEN_AUTHORIZED_SPEND") != "1":
323	            print(
324	                "[qgen] REAL mode needs PG_QGEN_AUTHORIZED_SPEND=1 (operator spend gate). "
325	                "Claude does NOT self-authorize spend. Aborting.",
326	                file=sys.stderr,
327	            )
328	            return 3
329	        retrieve = make_real_retrieve(domain=args.domain)
330	        judge = make_glm_coverage_judge()
331	        llm = make_glm_llm()
332	    else:
333	        retrieve, judge, llm = _stub_retrieve_and_judge()

1	"""Query-gen WINNER-DETERMINATION orchestrator (I-qgen-001, GH #1291).
2	
3	The single-task `run_qgen_coverage` proves coverage on ONE task; it does NOT determine the winner.
4	A real determination (operator directive 2026-06-23) needs three things the single run lacks:
5	
6	1. EQUAL BUDGET = the floor's OWN natural query count, per task. The floor for drb_72 fires 35
7	   queries (20 hand-authored amplified + 14 decomposed + anchor); an arbitrary cap (e.g. 24) would
8	   silently cripple it (the Codex iter-1 P1-6 failure). Both methods get the SAME cap = the floor's
9	   count, so the comparison isolates query QUALITY (whose N queries cover more), not query COUNT.
10	2. ALL rubric-backed DRB-II tasks (idx 56/62/66/72), not one — one task is one data point.
11	3. RERUNS (the GLM judge + closed-loop LLM are stochastic) + a paired bootstrap with a minimum-effect
12	   threshold, so the winner clears noise. Reruns reuse CACHED retrieval (retrieval keyed by query),
13	   so only the stochastic parts re-run — the heavy fetch is one-time per task.
14	
15	Faithfulness is untouched; this scores COVERAGE only. Retrieval + judge are the APPROVED harness
16	(run_qgen_coverage helpers + qgen_coverage_harness). Spend-gated behind PG_QGEN_AUTHORIZED_SPEND=1.
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import argparse
22	import json
23	import os
24	import random
25	import sys
26	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed
27	
28	from scripts.dr_benchmark.gate0_lineage import SLUG_TO_IDX
29	from scripts.dr_benchmark.qgen_coverage_harness import (
30	    CoverageBudget,
31	    load_blocked_references,
32	    load_canonical_question,
33	    run_coverage_test,
34	)
35	from scripts.dr_benchmark.qgen_methods import ClosedLoopMethod, FloorMethod
36	from scripts.dr_benchmark.run_qgen_coverage import (
37	    floor_queries,
38	    make_glm_coverage_judge,
39	    make_glm_llm,
40	    make_real_retrieve,
41	)
42	
43	# The 4 DRB-II tasks that carry an info_recall rubric (drb_90 has no gold task -> excluded).
44	DEFAULT_SLUGS = [
45	    "drb_72_ai_labor",
46	    "drb_75_metal_ions_cvd",
47	    "drb_76_gut_microbiota_crc",
48	    "drb_78_parkinsons_dbs",
49	]
50	FLOOR = "floor_template_facets"
51	CLOSED = "closed_loop_gap_requery"
52	
53	
54	def floor_budget(slug: str, question: str) -> int:
55	    """Equal budget for a task = the floor's natural query count (its facets + the anchor)."""
56	    return len(floor_queries(slug, question)) + 1
57	
58	
59	def run_one_task(slug: str, reruns: int, domain: str | None) -> dict:
60	    """Run BOTH methods on one task `reruns` times under the floor's equal budget.
61	
62	    Each task constructs its OWN retrieve/judge/llm (per-thread isolation — no shared client).
63	    Retrieval is cached by query, so reruns re-run only the stochastic judge + closed-loop LLM.
64	    """
65	    idx = SLUG_TO_IDX[slug]
66	    question = load_canonical_question(idx)
67	    blocked = load_blocked_references(idx)
68	    budget_n = floor_budget(slug, question)
69	    facets = floor_queries(slug, question)
70	
71	    retrieve = make_real_retrieve(domain=domain)
72	    judge = make_glm_coverage_judge()
73	    llm = make_glm_llm()
74	    # Equal cap = floor count; give the closed loop enough rounds to spend that budget adaptively.
75	    budget = CoverageBudget(max_queries=budget_n, max_query_rounds=int(os.getenv("PG_QGEN_MAX_QUERY_ROUNDS", "5")))
76	
77	    per_rerun: list[dict] = []
78	    for r in range(reruns):
79	        methods = [FloorMethod(facets=facets), ClosedLoopMethod(llm=llm)]
80	        results = run_coverage_test(idx, methods, retrieve, judge, budget=budget, blocked_refs=blocked)
81	        by = {x.method: x for x in results}
82	        per_rerun.append(
83	            {
84	                "rerun": r,
85	                "floor_coverage": by[FLOOR].coverage,
86	                "closed_coverage": by[CLOSED].coverage,
87	                "floor_covered": by[FLOOR].covered,
88	                "closed_covered": by[CLOSED].covered,
89	                "total": by[FLOOR].total,
90	                "floor_queries": by[FLOOR].n_queries_issued,
91	                "closed_queries": by[CLOSED].n_queries_issued,
92	                "floor_blocked_dropped": by[FLOOR].blocked_dropped,
93	                "closed_blocked_dropped": by[CLOSED].blocked_dropped,
94	            }
95	        )
96	        print(
97	            f"[determine] {slug} rerun {r+1}/{reruns}: floor={by[FLOOR].coverage:.3f} "
98	            f"closed={by[CLOSED].coverage:.3f} (budget={budget_n}q)",
99	            flush=True,
100	        )
101	
102	    floor_mean = sum(x["floor_coverage"] for x in per_rerun) / len(per_rerun)
103	    closed_mean = sum(x["closed_coverage"] for x in per_rerun) / len(per_rerun)
104	    return {
105	        "slug": slug,
106	        "idx": idx,
107	        "required_points": per_rerun[0]["total"],
108	        "equal_budget_queries": budget_n,
109	        "floor_mean_coverage": floor_mean,
110	        "closed_mean_coverage": closed_mean,
111	        "delta_mean": closed_mean - floor_mean,
112	        "per_rerun": per_rerun,
113	    }
114	
115	
116	def paired_bootstrap(deltas: list[float], iters: int, seed: int) -> dict:
117	    """Bootstrap CI of the mean paired delta (closed - floor). The independent unit is the TASK;
118	    we resample the per-task mean deltas with replacement. Honest about small N (4 tasks)."""
119	    rng = random.Random(seed)
120	    n = len(deltas)
121	    if n == 0:
122	        return {"mean": 0.0, "ci_low": 0.0, "ci_high": 0.0, "n": 0}
123	    means = []
124	    for _ in range(iters):
125	        sample = [deltas[rng.randrange(n)] for _ in range(n)]
126	        means.append(sum(sample) / n)
127	    means.sort()
128	    lo = means[int(0.025 * len(means))]
129	    hi = means[int(0.975 * len(means)) - 1] if len(means) > 1 else means[-1]
130	    return {"mean": sum(deltas) / n, "ci_low": lo, "ci_high": hi, "n": n}
131	
132	
133	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
134	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Query-gen winner determination (4 tasks, equal budget, reruns, bootstrap)")
135	    p.add_argument("--slugs", nargs="*", default=DEFAULT_SLUGS)
136	    p.add_argument("--reruns", type=int, default=3)
137	    p.add_argument("--workers", type=int, default=2, help="tasks run in parallel (I/O bound)")
138	    p.add_argument("--bootstrap-iters", type=int, default=10000)
139	    p.add_argument("--min-effect", type=float, default=0.05, help="min coverage delta to call a winner")
140	    p.add_argument("--seed", type=int, default=20260623)
141	    p.add_argument("--out", default="outputs/qgen_coverage/determination.json")
142	    args = p.parse_args(argv)
143	
144	    if os.getenv("PG_QGEN_AUTHORIZED_SPEND") != "1":
145	        print("[determine] needs PG_QGEN_AUTHORIZED_SPEND=1 (operator spend gate). Aborting.", file=sys.stderr)
146	        return 3
147	
148	    print(f"[determine] {len(args.slugs)} tasks x {args.reruns} reruns, {args.workers} parallel, equal budget = floor count/task")
149	    tasks: list[dict] = []
150	    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=args.workers) as ex:
151	        futs = {ex.submit(run_one_task, s, args.reruns, None): s for s in args.slugs}
152	        for fut in as_completed(futs):
153	            slug = futs[fut]
154	            try:
155	                tasks.append(fut.result())
156	            except Exception as exc:  # fail LOUD per task, keep the others
157	                print(f"[determine] TASK FAILED {slug}: {exc!r}", file=sys.stderr)
158	
159	    tasks.sort(key=lambda t: t["slug"])
160	    deltas = [t["delta_mean"] for t in tasks]
161	    boot = paired_bootstrap(deltas, args.bootstrap_iters, args.seed)
162	    floor_overall = sum(t["floor_mean_coverage"] for t in tasks) / len(tasks) if tasks else 0.0
163	    closed_overall = sum(t["closed_mean_coverage"] for t in tasks) / len(tasks) if tasks else 0.0
164	
165	    # Winner: the higher-coverage method, IFF the paired bootstrap CI clears the min-effect threshold
166	    # in its favor. Otherwise INCONCLUSIVE (do not crown a winner on noise).
167	    if boot["ci_low"] > args.min_effect:
168	        winner = CLOSED
169	    elif boot["ci_high"] < -args.min_effect:
170	        winner = FLOOR
171	    else:
172	        winner = "INCONCLUSIVE"
173	
174	    report = {
175	        "slugs": args.slugs,
176	        "reruns": args.reruns,
177	        "min_effect": args.min_effect,
178	        "seed": args.seed,
179	        "floor_overall_coverage": floor_overall,
180	        "closed_overall_coverage": closed_overall,
181	        "paired_delta_bootstrap": boot,
182	        "winner": winner,
183	        "tasks": tasks,
184	        "note": "Independent unit = task (N tasks). Small-N caveat: DRB-II has 4 rubric-backed tasks.",
185	    }
186	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(args.out), exist_ok=True)
187	    with open(args.out, "w", encoding="utf-8") as h:
188	        json.dump(report, h, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
189	
190	    print("\n=== DETERMINATION ===")
191	    for t in tasks:
192	        print(f"  {t['slug']:<26} floor={t['floor_mean_coverage']:.3f} closed={t['closed_mean_coverage']:.3f} "
193	              f"delta={t['delta_mean']:+.3f} (budget={t['equal_budget_queries']}q, {t['required_points']}pts)")
194	    print(f"  OVERALL floor={floor_overall:.3f} closed={closed_overall:.3f}")
195	    print(f"  paired delta mean={boot['mean']:+.3f} 95% CI [{boot['ci_low']:+.3f}, {boot['ci_high']:+.3f}] (n={boot['n']} tasks)")
196	    print(f"  WINNER: {winner}  (min-effect={args.min_effect})")
197	    print(f"[determine] wrote {args.out}")
198	    return 0
199	
200	
201	if __name__ == "__main__":
202	    raise SystemExit(main())

1	"""Resume-from-checkpoint query-gen determination — fast metrics (I-qgen-001, GH #1291).
2	
3	Resumes from the BANKED retrieval cache (the killed run's checkpoints) and scores floor vs
4	closed-loop with the two fast metrics in qgen_metrics:
5	  - breadth_recall  (no GLM, instant)         -> "which query-gen retrieves MORE distinct evidence"
6	  - finding_coverage (digest + parallel judge) -> "which covers MORE required findings"
7	
8	Both run per task under the SAME equal budget (the floor's natural query count). Default is a
9	TRUE resume (cache-only, no re-fetch); --allow-fetch fills partially-cached tasks. The blocked
10	DRB-II source is dropped before scoring (no cheating). Bootstrap over tasks decides the winner.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import argparse
16	import json
17	import os
18	import sys
19	
20	from scripts.dr_benchmark.gate0_lineage import SLUG_TO_IDX
21	from scripts.dr_benchmark.qgen_coverage_harness import (
22	    load_blocked_references,
23	    load_canonical_question,
24	    load_required_points,
25	    make_blocked_filter,
26	)
27	from scripts.dr_benchmark.qgen_methods import ClosedLoopMethod, FloorMethod
28	from scripts.dr_benchmark.qgen_coverage_harness import CoverageBudget
29	from scripts.dr_benchmark.qgen_metrics import (
30	    breadth_recall,
31	    build_digest,
32	    finding_coverage,
33	    make_cache_only_retrieve,
34	)
35	from scripts.dr_benchmark.run_qgen_coverage import floor_queries, make_glm_llm, make_glm_judge_llm
36	from scripts.dr_benchmark.run_qgen_determination import DEFAULT_SLUGS, floor_budget, paired_bootstrap
37	
38	FLOOR = "floor_template_facets"
39	CLOSED = "closed_loop_gap_requery"
40	
41	
42	def _build_corpus(method, question, retrieve, budget, is_blocked):
43	    """Run a method's queries against the (cache-only) retrieve, dropping blocked rows."""
44	    def filtered(query):
45	        return [r for r in retrieve(query) if not is_blocked({"url": r.get("url"), "text": r.get("text")})]
46	    return method.generate_corpus(question, filtered, budget)
47	
48	
49	def run_task(slug: str, llm, judge_llm, allow_fetch: bool, judge_workers: int,
50	             floor_only: bool = False, breadth_only: bool = False) -> dict:
51	    idx = SLUG_TO_IDX[slug]
52	    question = load_canonical_question(idx)
53	    points = load_required_points(idx)
54	    blocked = load_blocked_references(idx)
55	    is_blocked = make_blocked_filter(blocked)
56	    budget = CoverageBudget(max_queries=floor_budget(slug, question),
57	                            max_query_rounds=int(os.getenv("PG_QGEN_MAX_QUERY_ROUNDS", "2")))
58	    retrieve = make_cache_only_retrieve(domain=None, allow_fetch=allow_fetch)
59	
60	    out = {"slug": slug, "idx": idx, "required_points": len(points),
61	           "equal_budget_queries": budget.max_queries, "methods": {}}
62	    # floor_only resumes PURELY from cache (the floor's queries are deterministic + fully banked).
63	    # The closed-loop's gap queries are LLM-generated + NOT cached, so it needs live retrieval —
64	    # a separate fresh experiment, not a cache resume. The closed-loop's decompose/gap calls just
65	    # LIST search queries, so they get the reasoning-excluded LLM too.
66	    methods = [(FLOOR, FloorMethod(facets=floor_queries(slug, question)))]
67	    if not floor_only:
68	        methods.append((CLOSED, ClosedLoopMethod(llm=judge_llm)))
69	    for name, method in methods:
70	        corpus = _build_corpus(method, question, retrieve, budget, is_blocked)
71	        br = breadth_recall(corpus)
72	        entry = {"distinct_sources": br.distinct_sources, "distinct_findings": br.distinct_findings,
73	                 "total_rows": br.total_rows}
74	        if breadth_only:
75	            # NO judge — recall breadth only (distinct sources/findings at equal budget). Fast.
76	            out["methods"][name] = entry
77	            print(f"[resume] {slug} {name}: sources={br.distinct_sources} "
78	                  f"findings={br.distinct_findings} (breadth-only)", flush=True)
79	        else:
80	            digest = build_digest(corpus)
81	            fc = finding_coverage(points, digest, judge_llm, workers=judge_workers)
82	            entry.update({"digest_chars": len(digest), "finding_covered": fc.covered,
83	                          "finding_total": fc.total, "finding_coverage": fc.coverage})
84	            out["methods"][name] = entry
85	            print(f"[resume] {slug} {name}: sources={br.distinct_sources} findings={br.distinct_findings} "
86	                  f"finding_cov={fc.coverage:.3f} ({fc.covered}/{fc.total})", flush=True)
87	    if not floor_only:
88	        f, c = out["methods"][FLOOR], out["methods"][CLOSED]
89	        out["breadth_delta"] = c["distinct_findings"] - f["distinct_findings"]
90	        if not breadth_only:
91	            out["coverage_delta"] = c["finding_coverage"] - f["finding_coverage"]
92	    return out
93	
94	
95	def main(argv=None) -> int:
96	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Resume query-gen determination from cache (fast metrics)")
97	    p.add_argument("--slugs", nargs="*", default=DEFAULT_SLUGS)
98	    p.add_argument("--allow-fetch", action="store_true", help="fill partially-cached tasks (drb_76/78)")
99	    p.add_argument("--floor-only", action="store_true", help="score only the floor (pure cache resume; closed-loop needs fresh retrieval)")
100	    p.add_argument("--breadth-only", action="store_true", help="recall breadth only (no GLM judge): distinct sources/findings at equal budget")
101	    p.add_argument("--judge-workers", type=int, default=8)
102	    p.add_argument("--bootstrap-iters", type=int, default=10000)
103	    p.add_argument("--min-effect", type=float, default=0.05)
104	    p.add_argument("--seed", type=int, default=20260623)
105	    p.add_argument("--out", default="outputs/qgen_coverage/resume_determination.json")
106	    args = p.parse_args(argv)
107	
108	    if os.getenv("PG_QGEN_AUTHORIZED_SPEND") != "1":
109	        print("[resume] needs PG_QGEN_AUTHORIZED_SPEND=1 (the judge spends). Aborting.", file=sys.stderr)
110	        return 3
111	
112	    llm = make_glm_llm()              # closed-loop decompose/gap (reasoning ON)
113	    judge_llm = make_glm_judge_llm()  # finding-level coverage judge + closed-loop query gen (reasoning-excluded, fast)
114	    tasks = []
115	    for slug in args.slugs:
116	        try:
117	            tasks.append(run_task(slug, llm, judge_llm, args.allow_fetch, args.judge_workers,
118	                                   floor_only=args.floor_only, breadth_only=args.breadth_only))
119	        except Exception as exc:  # fail loud per task, keep the rest
120	            print(f"[resume] TASK FAILED {slug}: {exc!r}", file=sys.stderr)
121	
122	    if args.breadth_only and not args.floor_only:
123	        # Winner by RECALL BREADTH: which query-gen surfaces more distinct findings at equal budget.
124	        # Bootstrap over tasks on the per-task finding-count delta (closed - floor); no GLM judge.
125	        find_deltas = [float(t["breadth_delta"]) for t in tasks]
126	        boot = paired_bootstrap(find_deltas, args.bootstrap_iters, args.seed)
127	        f_find = sum(t["methods"][FLOOR]["distinct_findings"] for t in tasks)
128	        c_find = sum(t["methods"][CLOSED]["distinct_findings"] for t in tasks)
129	        f_src = sum(t["methods"][FLOOR]["distinct_sources"] for t in tasks)
130	        c_src = sum(t["methods"][CLOSED]["distinct_sources"] for t in tasks)
131	        if boot["ci_low"] > 0:
132	            winner = CLOSED
133	        elif boot["ci_high"] < 0:
134	            winner = FLOOR
135	        else:
136	            winner = "INCONCLUSIVE"
137	        report = {"slugs": args.slugs, "mode": "breadth_only", "seed": args.seed,
138	                  "floor_total_distinct_findings": f_find, "closed_total_distinct_findings": c_find,
139	                  "floor_total_distinct_sources": f_src, "closed_total_distinct_sources": c_src,
140	                  "finding_delta_bootstrap": boot, "winner_by_breadth": winner, "tasks": tasks}
141	        os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(args.out), exist_ok=True)
142	        with open(args.out, "w", encoding="utf-8") as h:
143	            json.dump(report, h, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
144	        print("\n=== BREADTH-ONLY DETERMINATION (distinct-finding recall, no judge) ===")
145	        for t in tasks:
146	            f, c = t["methods"][FLOOR], t["methods"][CLOSED]
147	            print(f"  {t['slug']:<26} findings floor={f['distinct_findings']} closed={c['distinct_findings']} "
148	                  f"(d={t['breadth_delta']:+d}) | sources floor={f['distinct_sources']} closed={c['distinct_sources']}")
149	        print(f"  OVERALL distinct findings floor={f_find} closed={c_find} | sources floor={f_src} closed={c_src}")
150	        print(f"  finding-count paired delta mean={boot['mean']:+.2f} 95% CI [{boot['ci_low']:+.2f},{boot['ci_high']:+.2f}]")
151	        print(f"  WINNER by breadth: {winner}")
152	        print(f"[resume] wrote {args.out}")
153	        return 0
154	
155	    if args.floor_only:
156	        f_cov = sum(t["methods"][FLOOR]["finding_coverage"] for t in tasks) / len(tasks) if tasks else 0.0
157	        f_find = sum(t["methods"][FLOOR]["distinct_findings"] for t in tasks)
158	        report = {"slugs": args.slugs, "mode": "floor_only",
159	                  "floor_overall_finding_coverage": f_cov, "floor_total_distinct_findings": f_find,
160	                  "tasks": tasks}
161	        os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(args.out), exist_ok=True)
162	        with open(args.out, "w", encoding="utf-8") as h:
163	            json.dump(report, h, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
164	        print("\n=== FLOOR (current POLARIS query-gen) — finding-level coverage from cache ===")
165	        for t in tasks:
166	            fm = t["methods"][FLOOR]
167	            print(f"  {t['slug']:<26} {t['required_points']}pts budget={t['equal_budget_queries']}q "
168	                  f"| sources={fm['distinct_sources']} findings={fm['distinct_findings']} "
169	                  f"| finding_coverage={fm['finding_coverage']:.3f} ({fm['finding_covered']}/{fm['finding_total']})")
170	        print(f"  OVERALL floor finding-coverage={f_cov:.3f}  total distinct findings={f_find}")
171	        print(f"[resume] wrote {args.out}")
172	        return 0
173	
174	    # Two rankings: finding-coverage (rubric-grounded) and breadth (raw recall). Winner needs the
175	    # coverage bootstrap CI to clear the min-effect threshold; breadth is the cross-check.
176	    cov_deltas = [t["coverage_delta"] for t in tasks]
177	    boot = paired_bootstrap(cov_deltas, args.bootstrap_iters, args.seed)
178	    f_cov = sum(t["methods"][FLOOR]["finding_coverage"] for t in tasks) / len(tasks) if tasks else 0.0
179	    c_cov = sum(t["methods"][CLOSED]["finding_coverage"] for t in tasks) / len(tasks) if tasks else 0.0
180	    f_find = sum(t["methods"][FLOOR]["distinct_findings"] for t in tasks)
181	    c_find = sum(t["methods"][CLOSED]["distinct_findings"] for t in tasks)
182	
183	    if boot["ci_low"] > args.min_effect:
184	        winner = CLOSED
185	    elif boot["ci_high"] < -args.min_effect:
186	        winner = FLOOR
187	    else:
188	        winner = "INCONCLUSIVE"
189	
190	    report = {"slugs": args.slugs, "min_effect": args.min_effect, "seed": args.seed,
191	              "floor_overall_finding_coverage": f_cov, "closed_overall_finding_coverage": c_cov,
192	              "floor_total_distinct_findings": f_find, "closed_total_distinct_findings": c_find,
193	              "coverage_delta_bootstrap": boot, "winner_by_coverage": winner,
194	              "breadth_winner": (CLOSED if c_find > f_find else FLOOR if f_find > c_find else "TIE"),
195	              "tasks": tasks}
196	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(args.out), exist_ok=True)
197	    with open(args.out, "w", encoding="utf-8") as h:
198	        json.dump(report, h, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
199	
200	    print("\n=== RESUME DETERMINATION (fast metrics) ===")
201	    for t in tasks:
202	        f, c = t["methods"][FLOOR], t["methods"][CLOSED]
203	        print(f"  {t['slug']:<26} findings floor={f['distinct_findings']} closed={c['distinct_findings']} "
204	              f"| coverage floor={f['finding_coverage']:.3f} closed={c['finding_coverage']:.3f} "
205	              f"(d={t['coverage_delta']:+.3f})")
206	    print(f"  OVERALL finding-coverage floor={f_cov:.3f} closed={c_cov:.3f}")
207	    print(f"  OVERALL distinct-findings floor={f_find} closed={c_find} (breadth winner: {report['breadth_winner']})")
208	    print(f"  coverage paired delta mean={boot['mean']:+.3f} 95% CI [{boot['ci_low']:+.3f},{boot['ci_high']:+.3f}]")
209	    print(f"  WINNER by coverage: {winner}  (min-effect={args.min_effect})")
210	    print(f"[resume] wrote {args.out}")
211	    return 0
212	
213	
214	if __name__ == "__main__":
215	    raise SystemExit(main())
216

45	# BROAD references-header (incl. Sources/Citations) — used ONLY by parse_references
46	# to find a source list to resolve citations against (Codex diff-gate P2-1).
47	_REFERENCES_HEADER_RE = re.compile(
48	    r"^\s*(?:#{1,4}\s*)?(?:\*\*)?"
49	    r"(references|sources|bibliography|works cited|citations)"
50	    r"(?:\*\*)?\s*:?\s*$",
51	    re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE,
52	)
53	# STRICT strip-trigger header — only a STRONG reference-list header (NOT "Sources",
54	# which is routinely prose like "Sources of evidence"). Used to DECIDE stripping.
55	_STRIP_HEADER_RE = re.compile(
56	    r"^\s*(?:#{1,4}\s*)?(?:\*\*)?"
57	    r"(references|bibliography|works cited)"
58	    r"(?:\*\*)?\s*:?\s*$",
59	    re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE,
60	)
61	# a numbered reference line "12. Author ... https://url"
62	_NUMBERED_REF_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*\[?(\d{1,3})[\].]\s+(.*)$")
63	_URL_RE = re.compile(r"https?://\S+")
64	# POSITIVE bibliographic-entry shape (Codex diff-gate iter3): a citation entry
65	# STARTS with an author-list signature — "Surname I." / "Surname, I. A." /
66	# "Surname & Surname" / "Surname and Surname" / "Surname et al" — or is a DOI line.
67	# A prose claim ("Semaglutide reduced cardiovascular events by 20% in 2020") lacks
68	# this author-initials/et-al structure and so is NEVER classified as a citation.
69	# A NEGATIVE (not-prose) test is leaky — a drug/proper-noun-led sentence dodges it;
70	# requiring the POSITIVE shape closes that. Erring here over-includes a few real
71	# references as atoms (the acceptable minor lane1 distortion) but NEVER drops prose.
72	# A surname is TITLE-CASE (capital then LOWERCASE): "Smith", "Acemoglu", "McKinsey".
73	# An ALL-CAPS acronym ("HPV", "DNA", "US", "AI") is NOT a surname — requiring the
74	# second char to be lowercase rejects acronym-led prose ("HPV DNA testing in 2020
75	# improved ...") that would otherwise match the author-initials branch (Codex
76	# diff-gate iter5 P1). A genuine reference's author is Title-Case.
77	_SURNAME = r"[A-Z][a-z][A-Za-z'\-]*"
78	_CITATION_SHAPE_RE = re.compile(
79	    r"^(?:"
80	    + _SURNAME + r",\s+(?:19|20)\d{2}\b"                  # Smith, 2020
81	    + r"|" + _SURNAME + r",?\s+[A-Z]{1,3}\b\.?"           # Smith J.  / Doe A,  / Smith, JA.
82	    + r"|" + _SURNAME + r"\s+(?:and|&)\s+" + _SURNAME     # Acemoglu and Restrepo / Smith & Doe
83	    + r"|" + _SURNAME + r"\s+et\s+al"                      # Smith et al
84	    + r"|doi:|https?://(?:dx\.)?doi\.org"                  # a DOI line
85	    + r")"
86	)
87	
88	
89	def _is_citation_entry(line: str) -> bool:
90	    """True ONLY for a positively-shaped bibliographic entry (author-initials /
91	    et al / "& Author" / "Surname, Year" / DOI) with a FRONT-LOADED year — after
92	    dropping any leading enumerator.
93	
94	    The front-loaded-year requirement (the year within the first 5 tokens) is the
95	    key discriminator that closes acronym-led prose (Codex diff-gate iter4 P1):
96	    a reference puts author+year up front ('Smith J. 2020 ...'), whereas a prose
97	    claim's year is embedded later in the sentence ('HPV DNA testing improved
98	    cervical precancer detection in the 2020 screening cohort'). A Vancouver-style
99	    reference with a late year is simply NOT stripped (kept as atoms = the
100	    acceptable minor over-inclusion), never a prose claim dropped."""
101	    s = re.sub(r"^\s*(?:\[?\d{1,3}[\].]|[-*•])\s*", "", line.strip()).strip()
102	    if not s:
103	        return False
104	    if not _CITATION_SHAPE_RE.match(s):                # positive author/DOI shape
105	        return False
106	    head = " ".join(s.split()[:5])                     # year must be front-loaded
107	    return bool(re.search(r"\b(?:19|20)\d{2}\b", head))
108	
109	
110	def split_body_and_references(report_text: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
111	    """Split a report into (body, references_section). Claims are extracted from
112	    the BODY only.
113	
114	    Codex diff-gate iter1+iter2 P1 (denominator-exclusion): strip the trailing
115	    block ONLY when (a) it follows the LAST STRONG reference header
116	    (References/Bibliography/Works Cited — NOT 'Sources', which is routinely prose)
117	    AND (b) EVERY non-empty trailing line is an unambiguous citation entry. If ANY
118	    line reads as a prose claim (numbered bullet prose, URL-bearing prose, etc.),
119	    the block is NOT stripped — fail-safe toward INCLUSION so prose claims can never
120	    be silently dropped from the denominator. (Over-including a true reference list
121	    as uncited atoms is the acceptable minor lane1 distortion, not a bypass.)
122	    """
123	    matches = list(_STRIP_HEADER_RE.finditer(report_text))
124	    if not matches:
125	        return report_text, ""
126	    m = matches[-1]
127	    after = report_text[m.end():]
128	    nonempty = [ln for ln in after.splitlines() if ln.strip()]
129	    if not nonempty:
130	        return report_text[: m.start()], after
131	    if all(_is_citation_entry(ln) for ln in nonempty):
132	        return report_text[: m.start()], after
133	    return report_text, ""
134	
135	
136	def parse_references(report_text: str) -> dict[str, str]:
137	    """Best-effort key→source map from a report's References section. Maps both a
138	    numbered key ("12") and any author-year-ish leading token to the first URL on
139	    the line (or the line text when no URL). Unresolved citations downstream
140	    correctly become UNREACHABLE(source_missing) — this parser never fabricates."""
141	    m = _REFERENCES_HEADER_RE.search(report_text)
142	    refs: dict[str, str] = {}
143	    if not m:
144	        return refs
145	    for line in report_text[m.end():].splitlines():
146	        line = line.strip()
147	        if not line:
148	            continue
149	        url_m = _URL_RE.search(line)
150	        resolved = url_m.group(0) if url_m else line[:300]
151	        num_m = _NUMBERED_REF_RE.match(line)
152	        if num_m:
153	            refs[num_m.group(1)] = resolved
154	        # also index by a normalized "Author, Year" leading fragment if present
155	        ay = re.match(r"[\[\d.\s]*([A-Z][A-Za-z]+(?:[^,]*,\s*(?:19|20)\d{2}))", line)
156	        if ay:
157	            key = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", ay.group(1).strip().lower())
158	            refs.setdefault(key, resolved)
159	    return refs
160	
161	
162	def _polaris_references(run_dir: Path) -> dict[str, str]:
163	    biblio_path = run_dir / "bibliography.json"
164	    if not biblio_path.exists():
165	        return {}
166	    try:
167	        data = json.loads(biblio_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
168	    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
169	        return {}
170	    refs: dict[str, str] = {}
171	    entries = data if isinstance(data, list) else data.get("entries", [])
172	    for e in entries:
173	        if isinstance(e, dict) and e.get("num") is not None:
174	            refs[str(e["num"])] = e.get("url") or e.get("evidence_id") or ""
175	    return refs
176	
177	
178	def run_scorecard(
179	    *,
180	    polaris_run_dir: str | Path | None,
181	    external_root: str | Path,
182	    span_fetcher: SpanFetcher,
183	    judge: Judge,
184	    atomizer: Callable[[str], list[str]] | None = None,
185	    rubrics=None,
186	    extended: bool | None = None,
187	) -> dict:
188	    """Extract -> FACT-score -> scorecard across all systems for the 5 locked Qs.
189	
190	    Returns the scorecard dict (lane1 + lane2_pending). The injected ``span_fetcher``
191	    + ``judge`` are the only billed work.
192	
193	    ``extended`` (I-perm-024 #1216): None (DEFAULT) → read the
194	    ``PG_BENCH_EXTENDED_METRICS`` env (unset → OFF → byte-identical pre-#1216
195	    scorecard); True/False overrides it explicitly (tests pass a bool). When ON, an
196	    additive ``extended`` block (the 5 claim-by-claim metrics + Claimify dedup) is
197	    computed per (system, qid) and attached — derived ONLY from the already-audited
198	    ClaimRows + the frozen rubric, never from raw report text."""
199	    if extended is None:
200	        extended = _extended_metrics_enabled_from_env()
201	    rows_by_system_qid: dict[tuple[str, str], list] = {}
202	    # (system, qid) -> list[ScoredClaim] (atom text paired with its ClaimRow), kept
203	    # ONLY when extended is on (the dedup needs the claim text). score_atoms emits
204	    # exactly one row per atom in order, so zip() pairs them faithfully.
205	    scored_by_system_qid: dict[tuple[str, str], list[ScoredClaim]] = {}
206	    # NON-SILENT reference stripping (Codex diff-gate audit trail): every block the
207	    # heuristic removes is RECORDED here so the §-1.1 audit / operator can verify
208	    # nothing material was dropped. A claim wrongly stripped is visible + catchable,
209	    # not a silent denominator bypass. (Follow-up: replace the regex heuristic with
210	    # judge-rated non-material exclusion so references need no stripping at all.)
211	    reference_stripping: list[dict] = []
212	
213	    def _ingest(system: str, report_text: str, references: dict[str, str], qid: str):
214	        # Extract claims from the BODY only — the References/Bibliography list is
215	        # not prose claims and must not be scored as uncited atoms.
216	        body, stripped = split_body_and_references(report_text)
217	        if stripped.strip():
218	            reference_stripping.append({
219	                "system": system, "question_id": qid,
220	                "stripped_chars": len(stripped),
221	                "stripped_lines": len([ln for ln in stripped.splitlines() if ln.strip()]),
222	                "preview": stripped.strip()[:300],
223	            })
224	        atoms = extract_atoms(body, system, references,
225	                              atomizer=atomizer, question_id=qid)
226	        rows = score_atoms(atoms, span_fetcher=span_fetcher, judge=judge)
227	        rows_by_system_qid[(system, qid)] = rows
228	        if extended:
229	            scored_by_system_qid[(system, qid)] = [
230	                ScoredClaim(text=a.text, row=r) for a, r in zip(atoms, rows)
231	            ]
232	
233	    external_root = Path(external_root)
234	    for system_dir, system in (("gpt_5_5_pro", "chatgpt"), ("gemini_3_1_pro", "gemini")):
235	        d = external_root / system_dir
236	        if not d.is_dir():
237	            continue
238	        for md in sorted(d.glob("Q*.md")):
239	            qid = re.match(r"Q(\d+)", md.name)
240	            if not qid:
241	                continue
242	            text = md.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
243	            _ingest(system, text, parse_references(text), qid.group(1))
244	
245	    if polaris_run_dir:
246	        run_dir = Path(polaris_run_dir)
247	        report = run_dir / "report.md"
248	        if report.exists():
249	            text = report.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
250	            qid_m = re.search(r"Q(\d+)", run_dir.name) or re.search(r"(\d+)", run_dir.name)
251	            qid = qid_m.group(1) if qid_m else "0"
252	            _ingest("polaris", text, _polaris_references(run_dir), qid)
253	
254	    extended_block: dict | None = None
255	    if extended:
256	        # Per-(system, qid) extended metrics from the audited ledger + frozen rubric.
257	        # JSON-safe string keys (tuple keys are not serializable).
258	        extended_block = {}
259	        for (system, qid), scored in scored_by_system_qid.items():
260	            nqid = normalize_qid(qid)
261	            rubric_elems = (rubrics or {}).get((system, qid)) \
262	                or (rubrics or {}).get((system, nqid))
263	            safety_ids = load_safety_floor_element_ids(nqid)
264	            extended_block[f"{system}:{nqid}"] = compute_extended_metrics(
265	                scored, rubric=rubric_elems, safety_element_ids=safety_ids,
266	            )
267	
268	    card = build_scorecard(
269	        rows_by_system_qid, rubrics=rubrics, extended=extended_block,
270	    )
271	    card["reference_stripping"] = reference_stripping   # audit trail (non-silent)
272	    return card
273

1	"""Render-time RUN-VALIDITY gates for the Gate-B benchmark harness (I-deepfix-001 loss-risk must-fix).
2	
3	Three pre-ship validity checks that make a paid benchmark run *actually valid* and fail CLOSED
4	before the report reaches the (expensive) scoring judge. They are the render-time half of the
5	loss-risk register FIX-2 / FIX-3:
6	
7	  FIX-2  QUESTION-FIDELITY — after render, assert the report's H1/title reflects the BOUND
8	         question and was NOT silently reformulated. The drb_72 disaster shipped a report whose
9	         title answered "Fourth Industrial Revolution / English-language journal articles only"
10	         instead of the canonical DRB-II GenAI-labor task -> info_recall 0/57. A reformulation
11	         phrase is a wrong-question tell ONLY when it is ABSENT from the bound question (the
12	         I-safety-002b program prompt legitimately contains those phrases; the canonical idx-56
13	         prompt does not), so every check is RELATIVE to the bound question, never a global string.
14	
15	  FIX-3  CONTRACT-SCAFFOLD — for a benchmark task whose gold prompt states an output CONTRACT
16	         (task72 = four named sections + a final 5-column summary table), assert the rendered
17	         report.md carries those sections + the table with the required header. A missing scaffold
18	         is a presentation-dimension loss the official judge scored 1/5. Config-driven from the
19	         per-task contract (``config/benchmark/task_output_contracts.yaml``), NEVER hardcoded to
20	         task72 — a slug with no contract entry is a NO-OP.
21	
22	DESIGN INVARIANTS
23	  * FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL: this module reads the already-rendered report + the bound question and
24	    decides ship / do-not-ship. It touches NO faithfulness gate (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 /
25	    provenance / span-grounding stay the only hard faithfulness gate).
26	  * §-1.3 WIDEN-ONLY: it adds a validity gate; it introduces no breadth cap / target / thinner.
27	  * NO SPEND / NO NETWORK / lazy heavy imports: pure functions operate on strings; the only I/O is
28	    reading the config file + the rendered report + writing a durable failure marker. Safe to import.
29	  * FAIL LOUD (LAW II): a violation raises ``RunValidityGateError`` — the run does NOT ship a report
30	    that answers the wrong question or drops the stated output contract.
31	
32	The pure predicates (``check_question_fidelity`` / ``check_contract_scaffold`` /
33	``evaluate_report_validity``) take plain strings + a contract dict so they are unit-testable with no
34	config file, no gold file, no network. ``enforce_render_validity`` is the I/O wiring the Gate-B
35	entrypoint calls after ``run_one_query`` returns.
36	"""
37	
38	from __future__ import annotations
39	
40	import json
41	import os
42	import re
43	from datetime import datetime, timezone
44	from pathlib import Path
45	from typing import Any, Optional
46	
47	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
48	# Config location (LAW VI: config-driven, cwd-independent, env-overridable).
49	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
50	_REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
51	DEFAULT_CONTRACT_PATH = _REPO_ROOT / "config" / "benchmark" / "task_output_contracts.yaml"
52	_CONTRACT_PATH_ENV = "PG_TASK_OUTPUT_CONTRACT_PATH"
53	
54	# The render-validity master kill-switch. DEFAULT ON (fail-closed behaviour armed for benchmark
55	# runs per the loss-risk directive); an operator may set it "0" to disable the render gates.
56	_GATE_ENABLED_ENV = "PG_RUN_VALIDITY_GATE"
57	
58	# The statuses that ship a FULL rendered report (so the contract/fidelity gates apply). An abort
59	# verdict artifact (abort_no_verified_sections / abort_corpus_approval_denied / abort_safety_refused)
60	# is intentionally NOT a full report — the gates skip it (nothing shipped to score).
61	SHIPPED_REPORT_STATUSES = frozenset({"success", "released_with_disclosed_gaps"})
62	
63	_HEADING_RE = re.compile(r"^\s{0,3}#{1,6}\s+(.*?)\s*#*\s*$", re.MULTILINE)
64	_H1_RE = re.compile(r"^\s{0,3}#\s+(.*?)\s*#*\s*$", re.MULTILINE)
65	# The generator titles a report ``# Research report: <question>``; strip that stock prefix so the
66	# title region is the actual question echo, not the boilerplate.
67	_TITLE_PREFIX_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*research report\s*:\s*", re.IGNORECASE)
68	# A rendered citation marker is the canonical ``[N]`` (the analyst-synthesis scrubber normalizes
69	# every marker to ``[<int>]`` and drops malformed forms). A line carrying one is CITED evidence
70	# (every verified sentence carries [N] per §9.1) — the FIX-13 framing scan EXCLUDES such lines so
71	# legitimately-cited body/table mentions of a phrase are never mistaken for a title reformulation.
72	_CITATION_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[\d+\]")
73	
74	
75	class RunValidityGateError(RuntimeError):
76	    """Raised when a rendered benchmark report fails a run-validity gate (fail loud, do not ship)."""
77	
78	
79	def run_validity_gate_enabled() -> bool:
80	    """Master kill-switch. DEFAULT ON — the fail-closed render gates are armed for benchmark runs.
81	    An explicit operator ``PG_RUN_VALIDITY_GATE=0`` disables them (LAW VI operator-override)."""
82	    return os.getenv(_GATE_ENABLED_ENV, "1").strip().lower() not in (
83	        "0", "false", "no", "off", "disabled", "",
84	    )
85	
86	
87	# The forbidden-reformulation FRAMING-ONLY switch (I-deepfix-001 FIX-13). DEFAULT OFF; ONLY the
88	# exact string "1" enables it. OFF => the reformulation scan keeps its verbatim body-wide substring
89	# containment (byte-identical). ON => a phrase counts ONLY when it appears in a FRAMING position
90	# (any heading OR an uncited body line) so 3 legitimately-CITED evidence mentions of the phrase no
91	# longer trip the gate (the drb_72 4IR false-positive that aborted a valid run), while a reformulated
92	# TITLE/heading and uncited generator prose adopting the phrase STAY fully detected.
93	_REFORMULATION_FRAMING_ONLY_ENV = "PG_RUN_VALIDITY_REFORMULATION_FRAMING_ONLY"
94	
95	
96	def run_validity_reformulation_framing_only_enabled() -> bool:
97	    """True iff the reformulation scan is restricted to FRAMING positions (headings + uncited body
98	    lines). DEFAULT OFF; ONLY the exact string ``"1"`` enables it (FIX-13 spec) so OFF => the
99	    verbatim body-wide containment => byte-identical. Read at call time (LAW VI operator-override)."""
100	    return os.getenv(_REFORMULATION_FRAMING_ONLY_ENV, "").strip() == "1"
101	
102	
103	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
104	# Contract loading.
105	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
106	def _contract_path() -> Path:
107	    override = os.getenv(_CONTRACT_PATH_ENV, "").strip()
108	    return Path(override) if override else DEFAULT_CONTRACT_PATH
109	
110	
111	def load_task_output_contract(
112	    slug: str, path: Optional[str | Path] = None
113	) -> Optional[dict[str, Any]]:
114	    """The per-task output contract for ``slug`` (or ``None`` if the slug has no entry / no file).
115	
116	    Returning ``None`` makes the gates a documented NO-OP for any benchmark task that does not
117	    declare a contract — the gate is general, never hardcoded to one slug.
118	    """
119	    contract_file = Path(path) if path is not None else _contract_path()
120	    if not contract_file.is_file():
121	        return None
122	    import yaml  # lazy: keep module import cheap + dependency-local
123	
124	    with contract_file.open(encoding="utf-8") as fh:
125	        data = yaml.safe_load(fh) or {}
126	    entry = data.get(slug)
127	    if not isinstance(entry, dict):
128	        return None
129	    return entry
