132	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
133	# Text helpers.
134	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
135	def _norm(text: str) -> str:
136	    """Lowercase + collapse whitespace for tolerant, case-insensitive containment checks."""
137	    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", str(text or "")).strip().lower()
138	
139	
140	def extract_h1(report_md: str) -> Optional[str]:
141	    """The first level-1 heading text (stock ``Research report:`` prefix stripped), or None."""
142	    m = _H1_RE.search(report_md or "")
143	    if not m:
144	        return None
145	    return _TITLE_PREFIX_RE.sub("", m.group(1).strip())
146	
147	
148	def _heading_texts(report_md: str) -> list[str]:
149	    return [m.strip() for m in _HEADING_RE.findall(report_md or "")]
150	
151	
152	def _table_header_rows(report_md: str) -> list[list[str]]:
153	    """Every GFM table header row (the row immediately followed by a ``|---|---|`` delimiter),
154	    returned as a list of normalized cell strings.
155	
156	    Only the leading + trailing bounding pipes are stripped; INTERNAL blank cells are KEPT so a
157	    header with a stray empty column does NOT silently collapse to the required column count and
158	    fail OPEN. A GFM row ``| A | B | C | D | E | |`` is a SIX-column header (last cell blank), not
159	    a five-column one, so it correctly fails an exact five-column contract (the Codex P1 fail-
160	    open where dropping the empty cell wrongly PASSED it)."""
161	    lines = (report_md or "").splitlines()
162	    rows: list[list[str]] = []
163	
164	    def _cells(line: str) -> list[str]:
165	        raw = line.strip()
166	        if raw.startswith("|"):
167	            raw = raw[1:]
168	        if raw.endswith("|"):
169	            raw = raw[:-1]
170	        return [_norm(c) for c in raw.split("|")]
171	
172	    for i in range(len(lines) - 1):
173	        line = lines[i]
174	        nxt = lines[i + 1].strip()
175	        if "|" not in line or "-" not in nxt:
176	            continue
177	        # A GFM delimiter row is composed ONLY of pipes / dashes / colons / spaces and has a dash.
178	        if set(nxt) <= set("|:- "):
179	            # KEEP internal blank cells (the bounding pipes were already stripped in ``_cells``);
180	            # a header must still carry at least one non-empty cell (a pure ``| | |`` divider is
181	            # not a header). A trailing/internal blank column therefore counts as a real column.
182	            cells = _cells(line)
183	            if any(cells):
184	                rows.append(cells)
185	    return rows
186	
187	
188	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
189	# FIX-2: question fidelity.
190	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
191	# A body UNIT is the smallest span the framing scan reasons about. Units are split on BOTH
192	# (a) a terminal ``.!?`` followed by whitespace AND (b) a newline — so a bullet, a GFM table row, a
193	# blockquote line, a numbered-list item, or ANY plain unterminated line each becomes its OWN unit and
194	# carries (or not) its own ``[N]`` citation marker. This is the Codex+Fable P1 fix: an UNCITED
195	# reformulation glued onto a FOLLOWING cited line that has NO terminal punctuation (bullets, table
196	# rows, plain unterminated lines) no longer inherits that line's ``[N]`` and slips through — each line
197	# now stands alone. ``\n+`` collapses a run of blank lines into one boundary.
198	_UNIT_SPLIT_RE = re.compile(r"(?<=[.!?])\s+|\n+")
199	
200	
201	def _split_sentences(text: str) -> list[str]:
202	    """Split a body region into UNITS on sentence terminals (``.!?`` + whitespace) AND newlines.
203	
204	    Each newline is a HARD unit boundary so a bullet / GFM table row / blockquote / numbered-list /
205	    plain unterminated line becomes its own unit (carrying its own citation status), while a ``. ``
206	    sentence terminal still splits two sentences that share one physical line. Pure, deterministic;
207	    empty units dropped. The consecutive-uncited-unit RE-JOIN that preserves a hard-wrapped phrase
208	    (``Fourth Industrial`` on one line, ``Revolution`` on the next) lives in ``_framing_violation``,
209	    not here — this helper only produces the units."""
210	    if not text:
211	        return []
212	    return [unit for unit in _UNIT_SPLIT_RE.split(text) if unit.strip()]
213	
214	
215	def _framing_violation(report_md: str, phrase_n: str) -> bool:
216	    """FIX-13 framing-only reformulation test. True iff the ALREADY-NORMALIZED phrase ``phrase_n``
217	    appears in a FRAMING position:
218	
219	      (1) ANY heading text (H1 title + every subsection / disclosed-gap header) — the original
220	          drb_72 disaster class (a reformulated title/section header) stays fully detected; OR
221	      (2) any UNCITED body UNIT (a sentence, bullet, GFM table row, blockquote/list line, or plain
222	          unterminated line that carries NO ``[N]`` citation marker) that contains the phrase.
223	
224	    Codex+Fable P1 gate-fix: the citation exclusion is UNIT-granular over units split on BOTH sentence
225	    terminals AND newlines, so an uncited reformulation on a bullet / table row / plain unterminated
226	    line GLUED before a following cited line no longer inherits that line's ``[N]``. Only a unit that
227	    ITSELF carries a marker is EXCLUDED as cited evidence — so N legitimately-cited evidence mentions
228	    (each its own cited unit) do NOT trip the gate, while an uncited reformulation can no longer slip
229	    through by sharing a line/glue-region with a cited claim. To preserve the hard-wrap catch (a
230	    phrase split across two UNCITED lines, ``Fourth Industrial`` / ``Revolution``), CONSECUTIVE uncited
231	    units are also concatenated and the phrase is checked on the joined run; a cited unit, a heading,
232	    OR a blank line BREAKS the run — so a phrase spanning an uncited→cited boundary is never joined
233	    (the drb_72 cited-evidence case that FIX-13 exists to pass). Headings are ALWAYS scanned (cited or
234	    not). Pure, no I/O; deterministic."""
235	    if not phrase_n:
236	        return False
237	    run: list[str] = []  # normalized text of the CONSECUTIVE uncited body units seen so far
238	    for line in (report_md or "").splitlines():
239	        heading = _HEADING_RE.match(line)
240	        if heading is not None:
241	            # (1) Headings are ALWAYS scanned regardless of any citation marker on the line; a
242	            #     heading also BREAKS any uncited body run (it is framing, not glued prose).
243	            if phrase_n in _norm(heading.group(1)):
244	                return True
245	            run = []
246	            continue
247	        if not line.strip():
248	            run = []  # a blank line / paragraph break BREAKS the uncited run (not a hard-wrap)
249	            continue
250	        # (2) Within a physical line, split into sentence units so a ``[N]`` marker excludes ONLY its
251	        #     own sentence. Each newline was already a boundary (line iteration), so a bullet / table
252	        #     row / plain unterminated line is its own unit and never inherits a sibling line's [N].
253	        for unit in _split_sentences(line):
254	            if _CITATION_MARKER_RE.search(unit):
255	                run = []  # THIS unit is cited evidence -> excluded AND it breaks the uncited run
256	                continue
257	            run.append(_norm(unit))
258	            # Per-unit AND consecutive-uncited-run containment: the run join reunites a hard-wrapped
259	            # phrase split across adjacent uncited lines (a single unit is simply the len-1 run).
260	            if phrase_n in " ".join(run):
261	                return True
262	    return False
263	
264	
265	def check_question_fidelity(
266	    report_md: str,
267	    bound_question: str,
268	    contract: dict[str, Any],
269	    *,
270	    framing_only: bool = False,
271	) -> list[str]:
272	    """Violations where the rendered report drifted from the BOUND question intent.
273	
274	    (1) A ``forbidden_reformulations`` phrase present in the report but ABSENT from the bound
275	        question is a wrong-question tell (the drb_72 title reformulation). When ``framing_only``
276	        is True (FIX-13, env ``PG_RUN_VALIDITY_REFORMULATION_FRAMING_ONLY``) a phrase counts ONLY
277	        in a FRAMING position (heading OR uncited body line) so legitimately-CITED evidence mentions
278	        no longer false-trip the gate; when False (DEFAULT) the verbatim body-wide substring
279	        containment is used (byte-identical to the pre-FIX-13 behaviour).
280	    (2) An ``intent_anchors`` group with NO hit in the H1/title region means the title does not
281	        reflect the bound question (or there is no H1 at all).
282	    """
283	    violations: list[str] = []
284	    report_n = _norm(report_md)
285	    question_n = _norm(bound_question)
286	
287	    for phrase in contract.get("forbidden_reformulations", []) or []:
288	        phrase_n = _norm(phrase)
289	        if not phrase_n:
290	            continue
291	        if phrase_n in question_n:
292	            continue  # legitimately in the bound question -> never a reformulation flag
293	        present = (
294	            _framing_violation(report_md, phrase_n)
295	            if framing_only
296	            else (phrase_n in report_n)
297	        )
298	        if present:
299	            violations.append(
300	                f"question-fidelity: reformulation phrase {phrase!r} appears in the rendered "
301	                f"report but is ABSENT from the bound question - the report was reformulated "
302	                f"(the drb_72 wrong-question class)."
303	            )
304	
305	    anchors = contract.get("intent_anchors", []) or []
306	    if anchors:
307	        h1 = extract_h1(report_md)
308	        if h1 is None:
309	            violations.append(
310	                "question-fidelity: rendered report has NO level-1 title heading to match "
311	                "against the bound-question intent."
312	            )
313	        else:
314	            h1_n = _norm(h1)
315	            for group in anchors:
316	                alts = group if isinstance(group, list) else [group]
317	                if not any(_norm(a) and _norm(a) in h1_n for a in alts):
318	                    violations.append(
319	                        f"question-fidelity: report title {h1!r} contains none of the bound-"
320	                        f"question intent anchors {list(alts)!r} - the title does not reflect "
321	                        f"the bound question."
322	                    )
323	    return violations
324	
325	
326	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
327	# FIX-3: output-contract scaffold.
328	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
329	def check_contract_scaffold(report_md: str, contract: dict[str, Any]) -> list[str]:
330	    """Violations where the rendered report is missing a stated section or the required table.
331	
332	    ``required_sections`` — each entry is a list of accepted heading synonyms; a section is present
333	    iff SOME heading line contains one synonym. ``required_table.columns`` — the report must carry a
334	    GFM table whose header row EXACTLY equals the configured columns after the same whitespace/case
335	    normalization (same count, same order, element-by-element ==); a substring, subset, reordered, or
336	    extra-column header is a contract violation (fail closed, not a substring/subset match).
337	    """
338	    violations: list[str] = []
339	    headings_n = [_norm(h) for h in _heading_texts(report_md)]
340	
341	    for section in contract.get("required_sections", []) or []:
342	        synonyms = section if isinstance(section, list) else [section]
343	        syn_n = [_norm(s) for s in synonyms if _norm(s)]
344	        if not any(any(s in h for h in headings_n) for s in syn_n):
345	            violations.append(
346	                f"contract-scaffold: required section {synonyms!r} is ABSENT - no heading "
347	                f"matches any of its accepted names (presentation-dimension loss)."
348	            )
349	
350	    table = contract.get("required_table") or {}
351	    required_cols = [str(c) for c in (table.get("columns", []) or [])]
352	    if required_cols:
353	        required_cols_n = [_norm(c) for c in required_cols]
354	        header_rows = _table_header_rows(report_md)
355	        # EXACT match: some GFM header row must equal the configured columns element-by-element
356	        # (same count, same order) after the identical ``_norm`` normalization both sides use. NO
357	        # substring, NO subset, NO reorder tolerance — an extra 6th column, a reversed order, or a
358	        # substring header ("Research Literature Notes" vs "Research Literature") is a violation.
359	        matched = any(row == required_cols_n for row in header_rows)
360	        if not matched:
361	            violations.append(
362	                f"contract-scaffold: required summary table with header columns {required_cols!r} "
363	                f"is ABSENT - found {len(header_rows)} table header row(s), none EXACTLY matching "
364	                f"the required columns (same count + order; presentation-dimension loss)."
365	            )
366	    return violations
367	
368	
369	def evaluate_report_validity(
370	    report_md: str,
371	    bound_question: str,
372	    contract: dict[str, Any],
373	    *,
374	    framing_only: bool = False,
375	) -> list[str]:
376	    """All run-validity violations (question-fidelity + contract-scaffold) for one rendered report.
377	
378	    ``framing_only`` (FIX-13) is threaded to ``check_question_fidelity`` only; it never touches the
379	    contract-scaffold check. DEFAULT False => byte-identical to the pre-FIX-13 behaviour."""
380	    return (
381	        check_question_fidelity(
382	            report_md, bound_question, contract, framing_only=framing_only
383	        )
384	        + check_contract_scaffold(report_md, contract)
385	    )
386	
387	
388	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
389	# I/O wiring: enforce on the rendered artifact, fail loud, do not ship.
390	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
391	def _write_failure_marker(run_dir: Path, slug: str, violations: list[str]) -> None:
392	    """Durable ``run_validity_gate.json`` marker + flip the on-disk manifest status to abort so a
393	    downstream scorer never silently ships an invalid report as a success (belt-and-suspenders to
394	    the raise). Best-effort: a marker/manifest write failure must NOT mask the fail-loud raise."""
395	    marker = {
396	        "verdict": "FAILED",
397	        "slug": slug,
398	        "gate": "run_validity_gate",
399	        "utc": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
400	        "violations": violations,
401	        "note": "report FAILED a render-time run-validity gate (question-fidelity / contract-"
402	                "scaffold); it must NOT be shipped to the scoring judge.",
403	    }
404	    try:
405	        (run_dir / "run_validity_gate.json").write_text(
406	            json.dumps(marker, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n", encoding="utf-8"
407	        )
408	    except OSError:
409	        pass
410	    manifest_path = run_dir / "manifest.json"
411	    try:
412	        if manifest_path.is_file():
413	            manifest = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
414	            manifest["status"] = "abort_run_validity_gate"
415	            manifest["run_validity_gate"] = {"verdict": "FAILED", "violations": violations}
416	            manifest_path.write_text(
417	                json.dumps(manifest, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n", encoding="utf-8"
418	            )
419	    except (OSError, ValueError):
420	        pass
421	
422	
423	def enforce_render_validity(
424	    summary: dict[str, Any],
425	    q: dict[str, Any],
426	    run_dir: Path,
427	    *,
428	    bound_question: Optional[str] = None,
429	    contract_path: Optional[str | Path] = None,
430	) -> Optional[list[str]]:
431	    """Post-render gate for one Gate-B query. Returns ``None`` when the gate does not apply (no
432	    contract for the slug / no rendered report / non-shipping status / gate disabled); returns the
433	    empty list when the report is VALID; RAISES ``RunValidityGateError`` when it is not.
434	
435	    ``bound_question`` defaults to the question the run actually answered (``q['question']`` — which,
436	    on the official-question-forced benchmark path, is the canonical DRB-II prompt). Faithfulness is
437	    untouched; this reads the shipped report + the bound question and decides ship / do-not-ship.
438	    """
439	    if not run_validity_gate_enabled():
440	        return None
441	    if summary.get("status") not in SHIPPED_REPORT_STATUSES:
442	        return None
443	    slug = q.get("slug", "")
444	    contract = load_task_output_contract(slug, path=contract_path)
445	    if not contract:
446	        return None
447	    report_path = Path(run_dir) / "report.md"
448	    if not report_path.is_file():
449	        return None
450	
451	    report_md = report_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
452	    question = bound_question if bound_question is not None else q.get("question", "")
453	    # FIX-13: read the framing-only switch ONCE here (env I/O stays in the I/O wiring; the pure
454	    # predicates take it as an explicit keyword). DEFAULT OFF => body-wide containment => byte-identical.
455	    framing_only = run_validity_reformulation_framing_only_enabled()
456	    violations = evaluate_report_validity(
457	        report_md, question, contract, framing_only=framing_only
458	    )
459	    if not violations:
460	        return []
461	
462	    _write_failure_marker(Path(run_dir), slug, violations)
463	    bullet = "\n  - ".join(violations)
464	    raise RunValidityGateError(
465	        f"RUN-VALIDITY GATE FAILED for {slug!r} - the rendered report is INVALID and must NOT be "
466	        f"shipped to the scoring judge:\n  - {bullet}\n"
467	        f"(marker: {run_dir / 'run_validity_gate.json'}; manifest status flipped to "
468	        f"abort_run_validity_gate)."
469	    )

2	
3	Loads a RECONCILED dual-§-1.1 audit ledger + the frozen-rubric JSON snapshot, runs the
4	existing two-lane scorer, and writes a per-(system, question) scored JSON.
5	
6	POLARIS-specific gate enforcement (Codex PR-3 design + Codex PR-2 P2 #2):
7	- If `<run_dir>/pathB_gate_INVALID` exists, refuse to score (the gate marked the run invalid).
8	- If `<run_dir>/pathB_gate_result.json` is missing OR verdict != "PASS", refuse to score.
9	- The gate is the SOURCE OF TRUTH for run validity; per-run artifacts (manifest, judge, etc.)
10	  may exist on disk even on FAIL, so the scorer MUST consult the sentinel + result first.

12	CLI:

16	        --ledger outputs/dr_benchmark/ledgers/polaris_Q75_reconciled.json \

166	            ))

224	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
225	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Path-B (system, question) scorer.")
226	    p.add_argument("--system", required=True, choices=("polaris", "chatgpt", "gemini"))
227	    p.add_argument("--question", required=True, dest="question_id",
228	                   choices=("Q72", "Q75", "Q76", "Q78", "Q90"))
229	    p.add_argument("--rubric", required=True, type=Path,
230	                   help="frozen-rubric JSON snapshot (rubric_v3_frozen.json)")
231	    p.add_argument("--ledger", required=True, type=Path,
232	                   help="reconciled dual-audit ledger JSON (claude+codex)")
233	    p.add_argument("--run-dir", type=Path, default=None,
234	                   help="polaris run dir (required for system=polaris; gate-check)")
235	    p.add_argument("--out", required=True, type=Path,
236	                   help="path to write scored JSON")
237	    args = p.parse_args(argv)
238	
239	    try:
240	        scored = score_one(
241	            system=args.system, question_id=args.question_id,
242	            rubric_path=args.rubric, ledger_path=args.ledger,
243	            run_dir=args.run_dir,
244	        )
245	    except InvalidRunError as exc:
246	        print(f"[score_run] INVALID: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
247	        # Write a stub INVALID record so the aggregator can still account for it.
248	        args.out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
249	        args.out.write_text(json.dumps({
250	            "system": args.system, "question_id": args.question_id,
251	            "passed": False, "invalid": True, "reason": str(exc),
252	        }, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
253	        return 2  # distinct from generic error
254	    except Exception as exc:
255	        print(f"[score_run] ERROR: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
256	        return 1
257	
258	    args.out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
259	    args.out.write_text(
260	        json.dumps(scored, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str) + "\n",
261	        encoding="utf-8",
262	    )
263	    return 0

122	# Probe token magnitudes (LAW VI, env-overridable). A liveness probe only needs to prove the route
123	# RESOLVES + the production call shape is ACCEPTED — NOT to emit a real verdict — so the per-role
124	# reasoning/output budgets the production body-builder sets (Mirror 24k / Judge 16k / decomp-Sentinel
125	# 16k) are clamped DOWN to a cheap, but STILL-VALID, magnitude. The clamp PRESERVES the production
126	# invariant that top-level max_tokens must exceed the reasoning budget (openrouter_role_transport
127	# lines 526-533); shrinking only the magnitudes keeps the provider/reasoning routing — the very
128	# `require_parameters`-filtered keys whose ABSENCE is the P1-2 false-pass class — byte-identical to
129	# production. Cost is driven by tokens actually GENERATED (a 1-token "ok" reply stops at EOS), not the
130	# ceiling, so this is ~free while remaining a faithful, routable body.
131	_PROBE_REASONING_TOKENS = int(os.getenv("PG_PREFLIGHT_PROBE_REASONING_TOKENS", "16"))
132	_PROBE_MAX_TOKENS = int(os.getenv("PG_PREFLIGHT_PROBE_MAX_TOKENS", "32"))

135	def _clamp_probe_budget(body: dict) -> dict:
136	    """Shrink the production body's token MAGNITUDES to a cheap probe size WITHOUT changing its shape.
137	
138	    Mutates ``body`` in place and returns it. Preserves every routing key the production builder set
139	    (``provider`` / ``reasoning`` enabled+effort or numeric cap) — those are exactly what OpenRouter's
140	    ``require_parameters`` filters on, so dropping them would re-introduce the P1-2 false-pass. Only the
141	    numeric budgets shrink, and they stay COHERENT with the production rule "top-level max_tokens must
142	    exceed the reasoning budget":
143	      - numeric reasoning cap (Mirror ``reasoning.max_tokens``): cap -> _PROBE_REASONING_TOKENS, and
144	        top-level max_tokens -> a value STRICTLY above it (cap + margin).
145	      - effort reasoning (Judge / decomposition-Sentinel ``reasoning.effort``): effort is KEPT
146	        (mutually exclusive with reasoning.max_tokens, NOT with top-level max_tokens), only the
147	        top-level max_tokens is lowered to the small probe ceiling.
148	      - no reasoning block (classifier Sentinel): just lower the top-level max_tokens.
149	    """
150	    reasoning = body.get("reasoning")
151	    if isinstance(reasoning, dict) and "max_tokens" in reasoning:
152	        # Mirror's NUMERIC reasoning cap path: shrink the cap, keep top-level strictly above it.
153	        reasoning["max_tokens"] = _PROBE_REASONING_TOKENS
154	        body["max_tokens"] = max(_PROBE_MAX_TOKENS, _PROBE_REASONING_TOKENS + 8)
155	    else:
156	        # Effort-based reasoning (Judge / decomp-Sentinel) OR no reasoning (classifier Sentinel):
157	        # effort and top-level max_tokens are NOT mutually exclusive, so keep the effort block as-is
158	        # and only lower the top-level ceiling. (Never set max_tokens=1 — a provider may reject it.)
159	        body["max_tokens"] = _PROBE_MAX_TOKENS
160	    return body

230	def _real_chat_completion_alive(
231	    role: str, slug: str, *, http_client=None
232	) -> bool:
233	    """One real, minimal completion POST on the verifier ``role``'s slug, built in the EXACT PRODUCTION
234	    CALL SHAPE for the active transport mode (``_build_probe_request`` reuses the production transports'
235	    OWN endpoint/auth/body construction). A dead slug (NoEndpoint / 404 / model offline / mis-routed) is
236	    a non-2xx -> ``raise_for_status()`` raises -> the caller maps it to GateError. The token budgets are
237	    clamped cheap, so it is ~free. This is NOT a catalog lookup and NOT a generic minimal body: it
238	    exercises the actual routing (endpoint path + auth presence/absence + provider/reasoning routing)
239	    the run uses — the drb_72 silent-dead-route + mis-route class the catalog preflight alone missed.
240	
241	    ``http_client`` is INJECTABLE (an ``httpx.Client`` or a ``MockTransport``-backed one) so the offline
242	    smoke drives the 404 -> GateError leaf with NO network; the live path passes None (a real client)."""
243	    import httpx
244	
245	    endpoint, headers, body = _build_probe_request(role, slug)
246	    own_client = http_client is None
247	    client = http_client or httpx.Client(timeout=float(os.getenv("PG_PREFLIGHT_PROBE_TIMEOUT", "30")))
248	    try:
249	        response = client.post(endpoint, headers=headers, json=body)
250	        response.raise_for_status()  # 404 / NoEndpoint -> HTTPStatusError -> GateError upstream
251	        data = response.json()
252	    finally:
253	        if own_client:
254	            client.close()
255	    # A routed-but-empty 200 still proves the slug RESOLVES (routing alive); content can be empty for a
256	    # 1-token probe. We only require a well-formed choices envelope.
257	    return isinstance(data.get("choices"), list) and len(data["choices"]) >= 1

264	    benchmark lineup on the OpenRouter route = the minimax Sentinel; the lock's self-host slugs on the

266	    ``_real_chat_completion_alive`` builds the request via the PRODUCTION transport's own helpers
267	    (``_build_probe_request``), so the probe hits the SAME endpoint path, the SAME auth presence/absence,
268	    and the SAME body/provider/reasoning routing the production verifier call uses (closes both P1-1
269	    self_host endpoint+auth and P1-2 openrouter provider-routing false-pass classes). Returns
270	    ``{role: slug}`` of the slugs proven alive; raises GateError on a slug that stays dead after the
271	    bounded 429/transient-transport backoff (fail closed only on a sustained NON-transient failure).
272	
273	    ``http_client`` is threaded into ``_real_chat_completion_alive`` so the offline smoke can drive the
274	    real dead-slug -> GateError leaf with a faked 404 transport (no network). Any endpoint/config
275	    resolution error (e.g. an unset self-host PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL, an absent OPENROUTER_API_KEY) raised by
276	    the production helpers is normalized to GateError — fail closed BEFORE spend."""
277	    from scripts.dr_benchmark.run_gate_b import verifier_model_slugs
278	
279	    slugs = verifier_model_slugs()  # {mirror, sentinel, judge: slug} for the ACTIVE transport mode
280	    alive: dict[str, str] = {}
281	    # I-wire-003 B2: under the concurrent benchmark fleet a verifier-slug endpoint is intermittently
282	    # 429-saturated. A 429 (or other transient transport error) means BUSY, not DEAD — fail-closing the
283	    # whole PAID run on a momentary rate-limit burst is a false alarm that kills good runs. Mirror the
284	    # sibling _default_credibility_judge_probe EXACTLY: tolerate a TRANSIENT (429 / rate-limit / timeout /
285	    # connection / overloaded) error with bounded exponential backoff; a NON-transient error (404 / auth /
286	    # model-not-found) still fails closed immediately, and a GateError still re-raises immediately. This is
287	    # a reachability probe ONLY — strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / span-grounding are untouched and
288	    # faithfulness is NOT relaxed. Bound is env-tunable (default 6 attempts: ~5+10+20+30+30s).
289	    _probe_retries = max(1, int(os.getenv("PG_PREFLIGHT_VERIFIER_PROBE_RETRIES", "6") or "6"))
290	    for role, slug in slugs.items():
291	        ok = False
292	        _last_exc: Exception | None = None
293	        for _attempt in range(_probe_retries):
294	            try:
295	                ok = _real_chat_completion_alive(role, slug, http_client=http_client)
296	                _last_exc = None
297	                break
298	            except GateError:
299	                raise
300	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — classified below
301	                _last_exc = exc
302	                _m = str(exc).lower()
303	                _transient = (
304	                    "429" in _m or "too many requests" in _m or "rate limit" in _m
305	                    or "rate-limit" in _m or "timeout" in _m or "timed out" in _m
306	                    or "connection" in _m or "temporarily" in _m or "overloaded" in _m
307	                )
308	                if not _transient or _attempt == _probe_retries - 1:
309	                    break
310	                time.sleep(min(5 * (2 ** _attempt), 30))
311	        if _last_exc is not None:  # transport / 404 / NoEndpoint / config failure -> fail closed
312	            raise GateError(
313	                f"super-heavy preflight: verifier role {role!r} slug {slug!r} is NOT alive in its "
314	                f"production call shape ({type(_last_exc).__name__}: {_last_exc}) after {_probe_retries} "
315	                f"attempt(s) — a dead/misrouted verifier route would silently degrade the 4-role gate. "
316	                f"Aborting BEFORE spend."
317	            )
318	        if not ok:
319	            raise GateError(
320	                f"super-heavy preflight: verifier role {role!r} slug {slug!r} returned no well-formed "
321	                f"completion envelope — the route is degraded. Aborting BEFORE spend."
322	            )
323	        alive[role] = slug
324	    return alive

327	def _default_credibility_judge_probe() -> str | None:
328	    """Probe the credibility judge slug via its OWN production sync caller, but ONLY when the credibility
329	    redesign is active in this run (matches production activation). Returns the probed slug, or None when
330	    the redesign is OFF (the judge is not called -> nothing to probe). Raises GateError on a dead slug."""
331	    if not credibility_redesign_active():
332	        return None
333	    from src.polaris_graph.authority.credibility_judge_caller import (
334	        credibility_judge_model,
335	        make_openrouter_credibility_caller,
336	    )
337	
338	    slug = credibility_judge_model()
339	    # I-deepfix-001 (#1344) cert-run #2 crash fix. The credibility judge (z-ai/glm-5.2) is a REASONING
340	    # model; the old probe forced max_tokens=1, which STARVES it — the reasoning tokens consume the cap,
341	    # so the call returns EMPTY (a false "alive", since the old code ignored the return value) on a fast
342	    # provider, or STALLS and trips the per-call wall-deadline on a LOADED provider, HARD-ABORTING the
343	    # whole paid run before spend. That is exactly the I-arch-002 / §9.1.8 "never starve a reasoning
344	    # model" lesson the PRODUCTION caller already encodes (max_tokens defaults to the 131072 chain-min) —
345	    # the probe re-introduced the starvation it guards against. Fix: a small NON-STARVING completion
346	    # budget so the high-effort reasoning completes and emits a real token (usage-billed; a trivial
347	    # answer is a fraction of a cent), and REQUIRE non-empty content (a route that returns nothing is
348	    # degraded, not alive). Env-tunable.
349	    probe_max_tokens = max(8, int(os.getenv("PG_PREFLIGHT_CREDIBILITY_PROBE_MAX_TOKENS", "2048") or "2048"))
350	    # A liveness probe FREE-ROUTES (OpenRouter picks the fastest HEALTHY provider) instead of pinning to
351	    # one mirror host. The credibility judge is ADVISORY — a dead/saturated route degrades the score to
352	    # neutral-weight in production (it NEVER drops a sentence), and the production caller itself rotates +
353	    # degrades — so a single transiently-saturated PINNED provider must NOT fail-close a $40 run before
354	    # spend (cert-run #2 hit precisely that: the pinned mirror lead was saturated ~5 min and the probe
355	    # re-hit it without rotating, because the caller's TimeoutError path does not rotate). We still hard-
356	    # abort when the MODEL is unreachable ACROSS providers (a non-transient 404/auth, or a sustained all-
357	    # provider transient). PG_ROLE_ALLOW_FALLBACKS is read lazily per-call by the caller; preflight is
358	    # sequential (no concurrent production calls), and the prior value is restored in `finally`.
359	    # Reachability probe ONLY — strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / span-grounding untouched; faithfulness NOT
360	    # relaxed. Bound env-tunable (default 6 attempts: ~5+10+20+30+30s).
361	    _probe_retries = max(1, int(os.getenv("PG_PREFLIGHT_JUDGE_PROBE_RETRIES", "6") or "6"))
362	    _last_exc: Exception | None = None
363	    _saw_empty = False
364	    _prev_free_route = os.environ.get("PG_ROLE_ALLOW_FALLBACKS")
365	    os.environ["PG_ROLE_ALLOW_FALLBACKS"] = "1"
366	    try:
367	        caller = make_openrouter_credibility_caller(max_tokens=probe_max_tokens)
368	        for _attempt in range(_probe_retries):
369	            try:
370	                out = caller("Reply with the single word OK.")
371	            except GateError:
372	                raise
373	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — classified below
374	                _last_exc = exc
375	                _m = str(exc).lower()
376	                _name = type(exc).__name__.lower()
377	                # Codex cred-probe gate P1 (#1344): the cert-run #2 failure is the caller's
378	                # concurrent.futures.TimeoutError (wall-deadline force-close), which stringifies EMPTY —
379	                # a message-ONLY classifier misses it (_m == "") and false-aborts after the FIRST attempt
380	                # instead of doing the promised bounded backoff/retry. Classify timeouts by TYPE/NAME too
381	                # (in Python 3.11+ concurrent.futures.TimeoutError IS builtin TimeoutError; the VM is
382	                # 3.11), so a transient timeout actually retries across the bound.
383	                _transient = (
384	                    isinstance(exc, TimeoutError)
385	                    or "timeout" in _name
386	                    or "429" in _m or "too many requests" in _m or "rate limit" in _m
387	                    or "rate-limit" in _m or "timeout" in _m or "timed out" in _m
388	                    or "connection" in _m or "temporarily" in _m or "overloaded" in _m
389	                )
390	                if not _transient or _attempt == _probe_retries - 1:
391	                    break
392	                time.sleep(min(5 * (2 ** _attempt), 30))
393	                continue
394	            if (out or "").strip():
395	                return slug  # route reachable AND produces usable output -> alive
396	            # Reachable but EMPTY (a degraded route / momentary blank-200 window): back off and retry
397	            # within the bound, like a transient.
398	            _saw_empty = True
399	            _last_exc = None
400	            if _attempt == _probe_retries - 1:
401	                break
402	            time.sleep(min(5 * (2 ** _attempt), 30))
403	    finally:
404	        if _prev_free_route is None:
405	            os.environ.pop("PG_ROLE_ALLOW_FALLBACKS", None)
406	        else:
407	            os.environ["PG_ROLE_ALLOW_FALLBACKS"] = _prev_free_route
408	    if _last_exc is not None:
409	        raise GateError(
410	            f"super-heavy preflight: credibility judge slug {slug!r} is NOT alive in its production "
411	            f"call shape ({type(_last_exc).__name__}: {_last_exc}) after {_probe_retries} attempt(s) "
412	            f"(free-routed across providers) — the credibility pass is active "
413	            f"({_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN_FLAG}) but its judge route is dead or sustainedly saturated. "
414	            f"Aborting BEFORE spend."
415	        )
416	    raise GateError(
417	        f"super-heavy preflight: credibility judge slug {slug!r} returned EMPTY content after "
418	        f"{_probe_retries} attempt(s) (free-routed across providers) — the credibility pass is active "
419	        f"({_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN_FLAG}) but the route produces no usable output. Aborting BEFORE spend."
420	        if _saw_empty else
421	        f"super-heavy preflight: credibility judge slug {slug!r} probe did not complete "
422	        f"({_probe_retries} attempt(s), free-routed). Aborting BEFORE spend."
423	    )

426	def _default_breadth_probe() -> int:
427	    """I-preflight-002 (#1169): run ONE real query through the PRODUCTION discovery functions and return
428	    the count of UNIQUE candidate URLs. This is the THROTTLE-REGRESSION signal — a wide run returns
429	    >= _PREFLIGHT_MIN_BREADTH; a silently-throttled run (back to ~40 URLs / single-page Serper) returns
430	    far fewer.
431	
432	    REUSES the EXACT production discovery functions the run drives — ``_serper_search`` and
433	    ``_s2_bulk_search`` (the same functions ``run_one_query`` calls via ``run_live_retrieval``), with the
434	    run's ACTUAL breadth budget read from the LIVE PG_SWEEP_* knobs (NOT the dead PG_LIVE_*-keyed
435	    ``DEFAULT_MAX_SERPER`` / ``DEFAULT_MAX_S2`` module constants, default 20 — keying off those would
436	    false-FAIL every wide run). Mirrors run_honest_sweep_r3.py:2271-2272 EXACTLY:
437	      - serper: ``num = PG_SWEEP_MAX_SERPER`` (default 12). ``_serper_search`` internally honors the
438	        ``PG_SERPER_TOTAL_PER_QUERY`` pagination budget from env, so passing the slate's MAX_SERPER
439	        reflects the wide config automatically.
440	      - S2: ``limit = PG_SWEEP_MAX_S2`` (default 12). ``_s2_bulk_search`` clamps to min(limit, 100).
441	
442	    NO fetch, NO LLM generation, NO classification: TWO search-API calls only (the canary already does
443	    one serper call). Returns the size of the UNIONED unique-URL set across both backends; the caller
444	    fails closed if it is below ``_PREFLIGHT_MIN_BREADTH``."""
445	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.live_retriever import (
446	        _s2_bulk_search,
447	        _serper_search,
448	    )
449	
450	    # Mirror run_honest_sweep_r3.py:2271-2272 — the LIVE breadth knobs, with the runner's defaults.
451	    max_serper = int(os.getenv("PG_SWEEP_MAX_SERPER", "12"))
452	    max_s2 = int(os.getenv("PG_SWEEP_MAX_S2", "12"))
453	    # The same high-volume probe query the canary's live-search probe uses (proves the THROTTLE CONFIG is
454	    # wide; it does not assert the run's actual question yields this many — that is the run's own corpus).
455	    query = "metformin efficacy in type 2 diabetes"
456	
457	    # I-transport-001 (#1191) Site 5 (FIXES drb_78): bounded retry around the Serper+S2 union.
458	    # `_serper_search` / `_s2_bulk_search` SWALLOW transient HTTP 5xx / timeouts internally and
459	    # return an empty list (live_retriever.py:410-431), so the retry trigger is "union still below
460	    # the floor," not an exception — re-issue BOTH backends each attempt so a transient S2 500
461	    # (0 S2 URLs on one attempt) is recovered on a later attempt.
462	    #
463	    # AC5 (Codex #1191 diff-gate, no-silent-downgrade): the floor is judged PER-ATTEMPT — each attempt
464	    # computes its OWN fresh union (one production-shaped query), and the gate passes iff a SINGLE
465	    # attempt clears `_PREFLIGHT_MIN_BREADTH`. We do NOT accumulate URLs across attempts: summing low
466	    # results from multiple flaky attempts would soften the throttle signal and could pass a genuinely
467	    # thin corpus that no single production query reaches. Retry recovers a transient backend ZERO; it
468	    # never sums partial results to reach the floor. We return the BEST single-attempt union (the most
469	    # protective true breadth signal). The caller's `_PREFLIGHT_MIN_BREADTH` floor check remains the
470	    # fail-closed terminal — this only prevents a single transient backend blip from aborting a
471	    # fundable run; it never widens the corpus floor.
472	    best = 0
473	    for attempt in range(_BREADTH_PROBE_RETRIES + 1):
474	        urls: set[str] = set()
475	        for hit in _serper_search(query, num=max_serper):
476	            u = hit.get("url", "")
477	            if u:
478	                urls.add(u)
479	        for hit in _s2_bulk_search(query, limit=max_s2):
480	            u = hit.get("url", "")
481	            if u:
482	                urls.add(u)
483	        best = max(best, len(urls))
484	        if best >= _min_breadth():
485	            break
486	        if attempt < _BREADTH_PROBE_RETRIES:
487	            logger.warning(
488	                "[super_heavy_preflight] breadth probe single-attempt union=%d < floor=%d after "
489	                "attempt %d/%d (likely a transient discovery-backend blip) — re-issuing both backends.",
490	                len(urls), _PREFLIGHT_MIN_BREADTH, attempt + 1, _BREADTH_PROBE_RETRIES + 1,
491	            )
492	            time.sleep(_BREADTH_PROBE_RETRY_BACKOFF_S)
493	    return best

496	async def _default_chromium_probe() -> None:
497	    """Reuse the FX-16 host-local fail-closed chromium probe (pg_preflight). FAIL -> GateError. A SKIP
498	    (DRY mode, or intentionally-disabled cascade) is treated per its reason: an intentionally-disabled
499	    cascade SKIP passes (the run won't browser-fetch); a DRY 'would FAIL in LIVE' SKIP is escalated to a
500	    GateError here because the super-heavy preflight is a PAID-RUN gate (fail closed on the run host).
501	
502	    ASYNC + AWAITED (NOT asyncio.run): super_heavy_preflight is awaited from run_gate_b_query's ALREADY-
503	    RUNNING event loop, so asyncio.run() here would raise 'cannot be called from a running event loop'
504	    and crash every live run (the SAME trap the canary's _default_structured_output_probe documents).
505	    The FX-16 pf.test_chromium_browser_available is itself a coroutine; await it directly."""
506	    import scripts.pg_preflight as pf
507	
508	    result = await pf.test_chromium_browser_available()
509	    if result.status == pf.PASS:
510	        return
511	    if result.status == pf.FAIL:

514	        )
515	    # SKIP: the cascade is intentionally off (passes) vs a DRY 'would FAIL in LIVE/paid' remediation
516	    # SKIP (escalate — a paid run must not proceed with a dead browser tier on the run host).
517	    if "intentionally off" in result.message:
518	        return
519	    raise GateError(
520	        f"super-heavy preflight: chromium probe SKIPped with a would-fail remediation — a paid run "
521	        f"must not proceed with a dead browser-fetch tier on the run host. {result.message}"
522	    )

526	    """RUNTIME re-assertion of the 5 recurring false-alarm regression checks. Imports the SHARED check
527	    logic from ``scripts.dr_benchmark.false_alarm_checks`` (a NON-test module) — NOT ``tests/`` — so the
528	    paid VM run never depends on ``tests/`` being importable. The pytest locks
529	    (tests/preflight/test_false_alarm_regressions.py) wrap the SAME five functions, so CI and this
530	    runtime gate assert identical conditions. An AssertionError (false alarm resurfaced) or an
531	    ImportError (logic moved) is normalized to GateError (fail closed). Returns the names that passed."""
532	    try:

534	    except Exception as exc:  # the shared check logic could not even be imported -> fail closed
535	        raise GateError(

537	            f"({type(exc).__name__}: {exc}) — the 5 durable kills cannot be re-asserted. Fail closed."
538	        )
539	
540	    passed: list[str] = []
541	    for check in ALL_CHECKS:
542	        try:

544	        except AssertionError as exc:  # a false alarm resurfaced -> fail closed
545	            raise GateError(
546	                f"super-heavy preflight: false-alarm regression lock {check.__name__} FAILED at run "
547	                f"time — a recurring false alarm has silently resurfaced ({exc})."

682	            f"SILENTLY THROTTLED back to a narrow corpus (the ~40-URL / single-page-Serper regression "
683	            f"class). The 1000-budget/STORM-wide path is NOT active. Aborting BEFORE spend."
684	        )
685	    summary["retrieval_breadth"] = n_candidates
686	
687	    print("SUPER_HEAVY_PREFLIGHT_OK", flush=True)
688	    return summary

1	"""Serving-identity probe for the 3 self-hosted verifier roles — I-meta-002 PR-8 / M2.
2	
3	Given the three role base URLs (resolved from env: ``PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL`` /
4	``PG_<ROLE>_API_KEY``), this probe queries each vLLM server's ``/v1/models`` and
5	asserts that the SERVED model id equals the role's locked ``model_slug`` from
6	``config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml``.
7	
8	Unlike ``openai_compatible_transport``, this probe targets self-host-only roles
9	and intentionally does NOT reuse that transport's ``OPENROUTER_API_KEY`` fallback:
10	the only auth source is ``PG_<ROLE>_API_KEY``, and when it is unset NO
11	Authorization header is sent (a self-hosted vLLM without ``--api-key`` needs none).
12	This guarantees the probe can never leak the OpenRouter key to a self-host box.
13	
14	This is the deterministic identity check the M4 ``served==pinned`` gate trusts:
15	before a paid benchmark run, the operator runs this probe to confirm each box is
16	serving the EXACT pinned model. ``/v1/models`` is the right primary vLLM identity
17	surface (Codex brief-gate P2): vLLM advertises its ``--served-model-name`` as the
18	``id`` of the single entry in ``data``. An optional 1-token chat completion is a
19	readiness-only nicety and is intentionally NOT performed here (it would spend).
20	
21	No-spend / no-network-in-tests boundary (LAW II): the HTTP client is
22	DEPENDENCY-INJECTED via ``probe_serving_identity(http_client=...)``. Tests pass an
23	``httpx.Client(transport=httpx.MockTransport(...))`` returning canned ``/v1/models``
24	bodies — there is NO network in any code path pytest exercises. The ``__main__``
25	entrypoint builds a real ``httpx.Client``; that path is run ONLY by the operator
26	during the paid canary, never by tests.
27	
28	Fail loud (LAW II): a served id that does not match the lock, an unreachable
29	endpoint, a malformed ``/v1/models`` body, or an UNSET ``PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL``
30	all raise ``ServingIdentityError`` (the probe never returns a silently-degraded
31	"reachable but unknown" success).
32	"""
33	
34	from __future__ import annotations
35	
36	import os
37	import sys
38	from dataclasses import dataclass
39	
40	import httpx
41	
42	from scripts.architecture.verify_lock import load_lock
43	
44	# The three self-hosted verifier roles this probe covers. The generator runs on
45	# OpenRouter (serving_route: openrouter) and is HARD-EXCLUDED — it is never probed.
46	SELF_HOST_ROLES = ("mirror", "sentinel", "judge")
47	
48	# OpenAI-compatible model-listing path appended to each role's base_url. This is
49	# the vLLM identity surface: data[0].id == the launched --served-model-name.
50	_MODELS_PATH = "/v1/models"
51	
52	# Per-role env var stems (LAW VI), identical to openai_compatible_transport.
53	# NOTE: no OPENROUTER_API_KEY fallback here. The probe targets self-host-only
54	# roles, so it intentionally does NOT reuse the transport's OpenRouter fallback —
55	# this prevents leaking the OpenRouter key to a self-host URL (Codex P2).
56	_BASE_URL_ENV_TEMPLATE = "PG_{role}_BASE_URL"
57	_API_KEY_ENV_TEMPLATE = "PG_{role}_API_KEY"
58	
59	# Reuse the shared LLM timeout knob (LAW VI): same env var, same fallback as the
60	# transport, so a probe and a real completion share one configured timeout.
61	_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = int(os.getenv("PG_LLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "90"))
62	
63	
64	class ServingIdentityError(RuntimeError):
65	    """A self-host serving-identity check failed loudly.
66	
67	    Raised on an unset base-url env, an unreachable / non-200 endpoint, a
68	    malformed ``/v1/models`` body, or a served model id that does not equal the
69	    locked slug. The probe NEVER returns a silently-degraded success.
70	    """
71	
72	
73	@dataclass(frozen=True)
74	class RoleIdentityReport:
75	    """Structured per-role result of the serving-identity probe.
76	
77	    ``served_model`` is the id vLLM advertises at ``/v1/models`` (its
78	    ``--served-model-name``); ``expected_slug`` is the lock-pinned ``model_slug``;
79	    ``matches_lock`` is True iff they are equal. ``reachable`` is True iff the
80	    endpoint answered HTTP 200 with a parseable ``/v1/models`` body.
81	    """
82	
83	    role: str
84	    base_url: str
85	    expected_slug: str
86	    reachable: bool
87	    served_model: str | None
88	    matches_lock: bool
89	
90	
91	def _expected_slugs() -> dict[str, str]:
92	    """Lock-pinned ``model_slug`` per self-host role (the expected served ids).
93	
94	    Sourced from the single machine-readable runtime lock via ``load_lock`` so the
95	    probe's expectations can never drift from the architecture lock (LAW VI).
96	    """
97	    lock = load_lock()
98	    required_roles = lock["required_roles"]
99	    return {role: required_roles[role]["model_slug"] for role in SELF_HOST_ROLES}
100	
101	
102	def _resolve_base_url(role: str) -> str:
103	    """Resolve ``PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL`` for a self-host role; fail loud if unset.
104	
105	    Mirrors ``openai_compatible_transport.role_endpoint``: a self-host role with no
106	    configured endpoint is a deployment error, never a silent default (LAW VI).
107	    """
108	    env_name = _BASE_URL_ENV_TEMPLATE.format(role=role.upper())
109	    base_url = os.getenv(env_name)
110	    if not base_url:
111	        raise ServingIdentityError(
112	            f"{env_name} is not set; the self-hosted {role!r} endpoint must be "
113	            f"configured before the identity probe can run (LAW VI)."
114	        )
115	    return base_url.rstrip("/")
116	
117	
118	def _resolve_api_key(role: str) -> str:
119	    """Resolve ``PG_<ROLE>_API_KEY`` only; return "" when unset.
120	
121	    Intentionally NO ``OPENROUTER_API_KEY`` fallback (unlike the transport): the
122	    probe targets self-host-only roles, so it must never send the OpenRouter key
123	    to a self-host box. When this returns "", the probe sends NO Authorization
124	    header (a self-hosted vLLM without ``--api-key`` needs none).
125	    """
126	    return os.getenv(_API_KEY_ENV_TEMPLATE.format(role=role.upper()), "")
127	
128	
129	def _served_model_id(raw: dict, *, role: str, base_url: str) -> str:
130	    """Extract the served model id from a vLLM ``/v1/models`` body. Fail loud.
131	
132	    vLLM returns ``{"object": "list", "data": [{"id": "<served-model-name>", ...}]}``.
133	    A non-object top-level body, a missing/empty ``data`` array, a non-dict first
134	    entry, or an absent ``id`` is a malformed identity surface and raises (via the
135	    structured ``ServingIdentityError`` path, not a bare ``AttributeError``) rather
136	    than returning a guess.
137	    """
138	    if not isinstance(raw, dict):
139	        raise ServingIdentityError(
140	            f"{role!r} {base_url}{_MODELS_PATH} returned a non-object body "
141	            f"({raw!r}); cannot confirm served identity."
142	        )
143	    data = raw.get("data")
144	    if not isinstance(data, list) or not data:
145	        raise ServingIdentityError(
146	            f"{role!r} {base_url}{_MODELS_PATH} returned no model list "
147	            f"(data={data!r}); cannot confirm served identity."
148	        )
149	    first = data[0]
150	    if not isinstance(first, dict):
151	        raise ServingIdentityError(
152	            f"{role!r} {base_url}{_MODELS_PATH} first model entry was not an object "
153	            f"({first!r})."
154	        )
155	    served = first.get("id")
156	    if not isinstance(served, str) or not served.strip():
157	        raise ServingIdentityError(
158	            f"{role!r} {base_url}{_MODELS_PATH} model entry carried no id "
159	            f"({first!r})."
160	        )
161	    return served.strip()
162	
163	
164	def probe_role_identity(
165	    role: str, *, http_client: httpx.Client, expected_slug: str
166	) -> RoleIdentityReport:
167	    """Probe ONE role's ``/v1/models`` and check served id == ``expected_slug``.
168	
169	    Fails loud (``ServingIdentityError``) on unset base-url env, transport error,
170	    non-200 status, malformed body, or a served id that differs from the locked
171	    slug. The ``http_client`` is INJECTED (tests pass a MockTransport stub).
172	    """
173	    base_url = _resolve_base_url(role)
174	    api_key = _resolve_api_key(role)
175	    url = f"{base_url}{_MODELS_PATH}"
176	    # Send Authorization ONLY when a per-role key is configured. A self-hosted
177	    # vLLM launched without --api-key needs none; we never send an empty or
178	    # foreign Bearer to a self-host box (no-leak — Codex P2).
179	    headers: dict[str, str] = {}
180	    if api_key:
181	        headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {api_key}"
182	
183	    try:
184	        response = http_client.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
185	    except httpx.HTTPError as exc:
186	        raise ServingIdentityError(
187	            f"{role!r} endpoint unreachable at {url}: {exc}"
188	        ) from exc
189	
190	    if response.status_code != httpx.codes.OK:
191	        raise ServingIdentityError(
192	            f"{role!r} endpoint returned HTTP {response.status_code} at {url}"
193	        )
194	
195	    try:
196	        raw = response.json()
197	    except (ValueError, httpx.DecodingError) as exc:
198	        raise ServingIdentityError(
199	            f"{role!r} endpoint returned a non-JSON body at {url}: {exc}"
200	        ) from exc
201	
202	    served_model = _served_model_id(raw, role=role, base_url=base_url)
203	    matches_lock = served_model == expected_slug
204	    if not matches_lock:
205	        raise ServingIdentityError(
206	            f"{role!r} served model {served_model!r} does NOT match the locked "
207	            f"slug {expected_slug!r} at {url}; the box is serving the wrong model."
208	        )
209	    return RoleIdentityReport(
210	        role=role,
211	        base_url=base_url,
212	        expected_slug=expected_slug,
213	        reachable=True,
214	        served_model=served_model,
215	        matches_lock=True,
216	    )
217	
218	
219	def probe_serving_identity(*, http_client: httpx.Client) -> list[RoleIdentityReport]:
220	    """Probe ALL 3 self-host roles. Raises on the FIRST failure (fail loud).
221	
222	    Returns one ``RoleIdentityReport`` per role when every served id matches its
223	    locked slug. The ``http_client`` is INJECTED so tests run with no network.
224	    """
225	    expected = _expected_slugs()
226	    reports: list[RoleIdentityReport] = []
227	    for role in SELF_HOST_ROLES:
228	        reports.append(
229	            probe_role_identity(
230	                role, http_client=http_client, expected_slug=expected[role]
231	            )
232	        )
233	    return reports
234	
235	
236	def _format_report(reports: list[RoleIdentityReport]) -> str:
237	    """Render a structured per-role identity report for the operator."""
238	    lines = ["serving-identity probe — per role:"]
239	    for report in reports:
240	        lines.append(
241	            f"  {report.role}: reachable={report.reachable} "
242	            f"served_model={report.served_model!r} "
243	            f"expected={report.expected_slug!r} "
244	            f"matches_lock={report.matches_lock}"
245	        )
246	    return "\n".join(lines)
247	
248	
249	def main(stream=sys.stdout) -> int:
250	    """Operator entrypoint — builds a REAL httpx.Client and probes all 3 roles.
251	
252	    Run ONLY during the paid canary (the boxes must be up). Exit 0 iff every
253	    role's served id matches its locked slug; non-zero (and a printed error) on
254	    any failure. NOT exercised by tests — tests call ``probe_serving_identity``
255	    with an injected stub.
256	    """
257	    with httpx.Client() as client:
258	        try:
259	            reports = probe_serving_identity(http_client=client)
260	        except ServingIdentityError as exc:
261	            print(f"FAIL: {exc}", file=stream)
262	            return 1
263	    print(_format_report(reports), file=stream)
264	    print("OK — all 3 verifier roles serve their locked slugs.", file=stream)
265	    return 0
266	
267	
268	if __name__ == "__main__":
269	    sys.exit(main())
270

1	"""I-deepfix-001 drb_72: prove the off-topic/relevance-conflict fix on the FROZEN box2 corpus.
2	
3	BUG: _is_confirmed_offtopic buried sources with content_relevance_label='relevant' (weight 1.0)
4	whenever topic_offtopic_demoted=True — burying the SEMINAL papers (GPTs-are-GPTs, World Bank,
5	Humlum). FIX (PG_OFFTOPIC_RELEVANCE_OVERRIDE, default ON): a positive content-relevance verdict
6	overrides the conflicting off-topic flag => KEEP.
7	
8	GREEN iff: with the flag ON, the seminal relevant-but-topic-demoted papers are NO LONGER
9	confirmed-off-topic (un-buried); a GENUINE off-topic row (demoted label, no positive relevance)
10	is STILL confirmed-off-topic; flag OFF reproduces the legacy (buried) behaviour byte-identically.
11	Deterministic, seconds, no LLM. Usage: PYTHONPATH=/c/POLARIS python <this> <corpus_snapshot.json>
12	"""
13	import json
14	import os
15	import sys
16	
17	sys.path.insert(0, os.getcwd())
18	from src.polaris_graph.generator import weighted_enrichment as we  # noqa: E402
19	
20	SNAP = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "corpus_snapshot.json"
21	rows = {r.get("evidence_id"): r for r in (json.load(open(SNAP, encoding="utf-8")).get("evidence_for_gen") or [])}
22	
23	# seminal papers: content-relevance RELEVANT but topic_offtopic_demoted=True (must UN-bury)
24	SEMINAL = ["ev_882", "ev_1018", "ev_1153", "ev_894", "ev_914"]
25	# genuine off-topic control: content_relevance_label demoted, NOT positive (must stay suppressed)
26	CONTROL_OFFTOPIC = [
27	    eid for eid, r in rows.items()
28	    if str(r.get("content_relevance_label", "")).lower() in ("demoted", "escalated_demoted")
29	    and str(r.get("content_relevance_label", "")).lower() not in ("relevant", "escalated_relevant")
30	    and r.get("topic_offtopic_demoted") is not True
31	][:5]
32	
33	fails = []
34	
35	
36	def check(flag_val):
37	    os.environ["PG_OFFTOPIC_RELEVANCE_OVERRIDE"] = flag_val
38	    seminal_buried = [e for e in SEMINAL if e in rows and we._is_confirmed_offtopic(rows[e])]
39	    control_suppressed = [e for e in CONTROL_OFFTOPIC if we._is_confirmed_offtopic(rows[e])]
40	    return seminal_buried, control_suppressed
41	
42	
43	on_buried, on_ctrl = check("1")
44	off_buried, off_ctrl = check("0")
45	
46	present = [e for e in SEMINAL if e in rows]
47	print("seminal present:", present)
48	print(f"FLAG ON : seminal still buried={on_buried}  genuine-offtopic still suppressed={len(on_ctrl)}/{len(CONTROL_OFFTOPIC)}")
49	print(f"FLAG OFF: seminal still buried={off_buried}  (legacy: these SHOULD be buried)")
50	
51	if on_buried:
52	    fails.append(f"ON still buries seminal {on_buried}")
53	if len(on_ctrl) != len(CONTROL_OFFTOPIC):
54	    fails.append(f"ON let genuine off-topic through ({len(on_ctrl)}/{len(CONTROL_OFFTOPIC)} suppressed)")
55	# the fix must MATTER: OFF must bury at least one seminal that ON keeps
56	if set(off_buried) <= set(on_buried):
57	    fails.append("no-op: OFF does not bury more than ON (fix has no effect)")
58	
59	print("\nRESULT:", "GREEN" if not fails else "FAILS=" + "; ".join(fails))
60	sys.exit(1 if fails else 0)
61

1	"""I-deepfix-001 wave-2 — PARALLEL-COMPOSE speed-lever proof harness (seconds-level, offline).
2	
3	Proves the connected-resume speed fix for the abstractive-writer pre-pass WITHOUT any fresh run /
4	fetch / GPU / LLM. The fix is:
5	
6	  * PRIMARY (env-only, already-existing knobs): PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY (default 8) and
7	    PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_WALL_DEADLINE_S (default 720) read at
8	    src/polaris_graph/generator/abstractive_writer.py:639-640. Raising concurrency to 16 fans the
9	    pre-pass out wider (WHEN, not WHAT); raising the wall to 3600 stops a healthy-but-uniformly-slow
10	    run from abandoning its last wave to K-span.
11	  * SECONDARY (small code change): the OpenRouter 429 backoff now HONORS a server Retry-After header
12	    (src/polaris_graph/llm/openrouter_client.py, new _parse_retry_after + the 429 branch), so a
13	    rate-limited writer call recovers at the window the server names instead of the blind 15/30/60s
14	    floor. Absent header => byte-identical to the old floor-only behaviour.
15	
16	Four assertions (GREEN iff all pass AND the OFF/default path is byte-identical):
17	  (a) DETERMINISM: the precomputed-draft dict is IDENTICAL across concurrency 1 / 8 / 16 / default
18	      — concurrency changes WHEN the drafts are computed, never WHAT they are.
19	  (b) SPEEDUP: with a 0.5s-per-basket stub and >=32 baskets, wall(concurrency=16) < 0.2 * wall(1).
20	  (c) ZERO ABANDONMENT: with WALL_DEADLINE_S=3600 every basket is drafted and nothing is abandoned;
21	      the contrast (tiny wall) proves the wall lever is real and that 3600 is what suppresses it.
22	      Also: the healthy output at the DEFAULT wall (720) is byte-identical to the 3600 output.
23	  (d) 429 + RETRY-AFTER: a synthetic 429 on the first transport call (then success) recovers through
24	      the REAL OpenRouterClient retry branch — no exception escapes, content is returned, and the
25	      slept delay equals the honored Retry-After (not the 15s floor); with no header the delay is the
26	      old 15s value (byte-identical). Plus the pre-pass collects every recovered basket into `out`.
27	
28	Run:  PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 PYTHONPATH=<worktree-root> python scripts/dr_benchmark/wave2_parallel_prepass.py
29	
30	Faithfulness: touches only transport timing (concurrency / wall / retry backoff). strict_verify /
31	NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding are never imported or called by the fix or this harness.
32	"""
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	import asyncio
36	import logging
37	import os
38	import sys
39	import time
40	from pathlib import Path
41	
42	# Make `src...` importable even without an explicit PYTHONPATH (worktree root = parents[2]).
43	_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
44	if str(_ROOT) not in sys.path:
45	    sys.path.insert(0, str(_ROOT))
46	
47	# A dummy key so OpenRouterClient can be CONSTRUCTED offline (its transport is stubbed; no network).
48	os.environ.setdefault("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "harness-dummy-key-not-used")
49	
50	import httpx  # noqa: E402
51	
52	from src.polaris_graph.generator import abstractive_writer as aw  # noqa: E402
53	from src.polaris_graph.llm import openrouter_client as oc  # noqa: E402
54	
55	N_BASKETS = 32
56	PER_BASKET_SLEEP_S = 0.5
57	
58	_CONC_ENV = "PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY"
59	_WALL_ENV = "PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_WALL_DEADLINE_S"
60	
61	
62	class _Basket:
63	    """Minimal synthetic basket — the real pre-pass keys the precomputed dict on
64	    ``claim_cluster_id`` (abstractive_writer._basket_key), which is all the orchestration needs."""
65	
66	    def __init__(self, cid: str) -> None:
67	        self.claim_cluster_id = cid
68	
69	
70	class _StubVerification:
71	    """A stub SentenceVerification-shaped result (always verified). Provided as the STUB
72	    writer_verify_fn per the task; the drafting-orchestration tests stub the per-basket unit so it is
73	    not exercised there, but abstractive_pre_pass requires the argument."""
74	
75	    is_verified = True
76	    judge_error = False
77	    failure_reasons: list = []
78	
79	    def __init__(self, sentence: str) -> None:
80	        self.sentence = sentence
81	
82	
83	def _stub_verify_fn(sentence: str, scoped_pool: dict, *args, **kwargs) -> _StubVerification:
84	    return _StubVerification(sentence)
85	
86	
87	class _LogCapture(logging.Handler):
88	    def __init__(self) -> None:
89	        super().__init__(level=logging.DEBUG)
90	        self.records: list[str] = []
91	
92	    def emit(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> None:
93	        try:
94	            self.records.append(record.getMessage())
95	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
96	            self.records.append(str(record.msg))
97	
98	    def has(self, needle: str) -> bool:
99	        return any(needle in m for m in self.records)
100	
101	
102	def _make_baskets(n: int) -> list:
103	    return [_Basket(f"cid-{i:03d}") for i in range(n)]
104	
105	
106	def _deterministic_draft(basket) -> str:
107	    # Depends ONLY on the basket key -> identical regardless of concurrency / scheduling order.
108	    return f"VERIFIED_DRAFT::{basket.claim_cluster_id}"
109	
110	
111	def _install_drafting_stub(sleep_s: float):
112	    """Replace the per-basket unit with a controllable stub so abstractive_pre_pass's REAL semaphore /
113	    wall / abandon orchestration is what runs. Returns the original for restoration."""
114	
115	    original = aw._pre_pass_one_basket
116	
117	    async def _stub(basket, evidence_pool, **kwargs):  # noqa: ARG001 — mirrors real kw-only signature
118	        await asyncio.sleep(sleep_s)
119	        return _deterministic_draft(basket)
120	
121	    aw._pre_pass_one_basket = _stub  # type: ignore[assignment]
122	    return original
123	
124	
125	def _run_prepass(baskets, *, concurrency, wall_s, sleep_s=PER_BASKET_SLEEP_S):
126	    """Run abstractive_pre_pass once with the given knobs; return (out_dict, wall_seconds, log_lines).
127	
128	    concurrency / wall_s == None => leave the env UNSET so the module DEFAULT (8 / 720) applies."""
129	    for env_name, value in ((_CONC_ENV, concurrency), (_WALL_ENV, wall_s)):
130	        if value is None:
131	            os.environ.pop(env_name, None)
132	        else:
133	            os.environ[env_name] = str(value)
134	
135	    cap = _LogCapture()
136	    # Route the module logger ONLY to our capture handler (level DEBUG so nothing is gated, propagate
137	    # False so records never reach the root/stderr "last resort" handler). Global logging.disable would
138	    # suppress the record before the handler ever sees it, so we deliberately do NOT use it here.
139	    prev_level, prev_propagate = aw.logger.level, aw.logger.propagate
140	    aw.logger.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)
141	    aw.logger.propagate = False
142	    aw.logger.addHandler(cap)
143	    aw._DETACHED_WRITER_TASKS.clear()
144	    original = _install_drafting_stub(sleep_s)
145	    try:
146	        t0 = time.perf_counter()
147	        out = asyncio.run(
148	            aw.abstractive_pre_pass(baskets, {}, writer_verify_fn=_stub_verify_fn)
149	        )
150	        wall = time.perf_counter() - t0
151	    finally:
152	        aw._pre_pass_one_basket = original  # type: ignore[assignment]
153	        aw.logger.removeHandler(cap)
154	        aw.logger.setLevel(prev_level)
155	        aw.logger.propagate = prev_propagate

244	def _make_read_stream(client, scenario_header):
245	    """A fake OpenRouterClient._read_stream: raise a synthetic 429 (with the given Retry-After header,
246	    or none) on the FIRST call, then return a healthy stream tuple. Returns (fake, state)."""
247	    state = {"calls": 0}
248	
249	    async def _fake(body, actual_timeout):  # noqa: ARG001
250	        state["calls"] += 1
251	        if state["calls"] == 1:
252	            req = httpx.Request("POST", "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions")
253	            headers = {} if scenario_header is None else {"Retry-After": scenario_header}
254	            resp = httpx.Response(429, headers=headers, request=req)
255	            raise httpx.HTTPStatusError("429 Too Many Requests", request=req, response=resp)
256	        # (content, reasoning, usage, served) — the shape the stream branch unpacks.
257	        return (
258	            "Weight fell by 15 percent. [#ev:e1:0-25]",
259	            "",
260	            {"finish_reason": "stop", "prompt_tokens": 20,
261	             "completion_tokens": 10, "total_tokens": 30, "cost": 0.0},
262	            {"provider": "stub-provider", "model": client.model},
263	        )
264	
265	    return _fake, state

268	def _run_client_429_scenario(scenario_header):
269	    """Drive the REAL OpenRouterClient.generate through one injected 429 -> success, recording every
270	    asyncio.sleep. Returns (content, calls, recorded_sleeps, escaped_exc)."""
271	    recorded: list[float] = []
272	    orig_sleep = asyncio.sleep
273	
274	    async def _rec_sleep(delay, *a, **k):  # record + do not actually block
275	        recorded.append(float(delay))
276	
277	    async def _drive():
278	        client = oc.OpenRouterClient(model="z-ai/glm-5.2")
279	        fake, state = _make_read_stream(client, scenario_header)
280	        client._read_stream = fake  # instance attr shadows the bound method
281	        try:
282	            resp = await client.generate(prompt="rephrase the span", system="be faithful",
283	                                         max_tokens=256, temperature=0.2)
284	            return getattr(resp, "content", ""), state["calls"], None
285	        finally:
286	            await client.close()
287	
288	    asyncio.sleep = _rec_sleep  # type: ignore[assignment]
289	    try:
290	        content, calls, esc = asyncio.run(_drive())
291	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a real escape is a RED signal, captured not hidden
292	        content, calls, esc = "", -1, exc
293	    finally:
294	        asyncio.sleep = orig_sleep  # type: ignore[assignment]
295	    return content, calls, recorded, esc
296

298	def test_client_429_retry_after() -> dict:

303	    honored3 = (esc3 is None) and bool(c3) and (calls3 == 2) and (3.0 in sleeps3) and (15.0 not in sleeps3)
304	    results["d_client_retry_after_3s"] = honored3
305	    results["_d_3s_detail"] = f"content={bool(c3)} calls={calls3} sleeps={sleeps3} escaped={esc3!r}"
306	
307	    # Retry-After = 90s -> honored (exceeds the OLD 60s cap, proving the cap was raised for the signal).
308	    c90, calls90, sleeps90, esc90 = _run_client_429_scenario("90")
309	    honored90 = (esc90 is None) and bool(c90) and (90.0 in sleeps90)
310	    results["d_client_retry_after_90s"] = honored90
311	    results["_d_90s_detail"] = f"content={bool(c90)} calls={calls90} sleeps={sleeps90} escaped={esc90!r}"
312	
313	    # No header -> byte-identical to the old floor-only behaviour: attempt-0 wait == 15.0s.
314	    cN, callsN, sleepsN, escN = _run_client_429_scenario(None)
315	    control_ok = (escN is None) and bool(cN) and (sleepsN == [15.0])
316	    results["d_client_no_header_byte_identical"] = control_ok
317	    results["_d_control_detail"] = f"content={bool(cN)} calls={callsN} sleeps={sleepsN} escaped={escN!r}"
318	    return results

321	def test_prepass_all_baskets_draft_under_429() -> dict:
322	    """Pre-pass layer of (d): when the writer call recovers from a 429 (proven real by the client test
323	    above), abstractive_pre_pass collects EVERY basket into `out` and no exception escapes. The stub
324	    invokes the REAL oc._parse_retry_after on a synthetic header to show the honored delay is computed
325	    by production code, then returns the recovered draft."""
326	    baskets = _make_baskets(12)
327	    honored: list = []
328	    original = aw._pre_pass_one_basket
329	
330	    async def _recovering(basket, evidence_pool, **kwargs):  # noqa: ARG001
331	        # models: transport raised a 429 carrying Retry-After: 3; the client honored+recovered.
332	        honored.append(oc._parse_retry_after("3"))
333	        await asyncio.sleep(0.0)
334	        return _deterministic_draft(basket)
335	
336	    os.environ[_WALL_ENV] = "3600"
337	    os.environ[_CONC_ENV] = "16"
338	    aw._pre_pass_one_basket = _recovering  # type: ignore[assignment]
339	    escaped = None
340	    try:
341	        out = asyncio.run(aw.abstractive_pre_pass(baskets, {}, writer_verify_fn=_stub_verify_fn))
342	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
343	        out, escaped = {}, exc
344	    finally:
345	        aw._pre_pass_one_basket = original  # type: ignore[assignment]
346	
347	    ok = (escaped is None) and (len(out) == 12) and all(v == 3.0 for v in honored) and len(honored) == 12
348	    return {
349	        "d_prepass_all_draft_under_429": ok,
350	        "_d_prepass_detail": f"drafted={len(out)}/12 honored_delays={set(honored)} escaped={escaped!r}",
351	    }

354	def main() -> int:
355	    # Silence root/other-module noise on stderr WITHOUT using logging.disable (which would also gate
356	    # the pre-pass capture handler). The pre-pass logger is routed to its capture handler with
357	    # propagate=False inside _run_prepass; everything else inherits this CRITICAL root level.
358	    logging.getLogger().setLevel(logging.CRITICAL)
359	    results: dict = {}

361	    results.update(test_parse_retry_after_unit())
362	    results.update(test_client_429_retry_after())
363	    results.update(test_prepass_all_baskets_draft_under_429())
364	
365	    assertion_keys = [k for k in results if not k.startswith("_")]
366	    print("=" * 78)
367	    print("I-deepfix-001 wave-2 — PARALLEL-COMPOSE speed-lever proof")
368	    print("=" * 78)

380	    print(f"assertions passed: {sum(1 for k in assertion_keys if results[k])}/{len(assertion_keys)}")

22	# Extract claim UNITS: top-level "- " bullets, and prose sentences from body paragraphs.
